title: 2nd to 4th Year Pipefitting & Plumbing Apprentices
description: ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Scope of the Role:** Mercury is seeking **2nd to 4th Year Pipefitting \& Plumbing Apprentices** to join us, working within Dublin and the greater Dublin region. Our apprenticeship opportunity offers a structured pathway through its seven phases and inhouse development programs, leading to potential management \& professional roles upon completion, across various sectors of the business. Prior experience is necessary. During the apprenticeship, Mercury will provide additional training in our dedicated Apprentice Development Centre in Dublin. Key Duties \& Responsibilities: * Reports to a Foreperson * Work Schedule: 39 hours per week \& overtime when requested. * Apprenticeships are governed by SOLAS and are standards based. During your apprenticeship, you will be required to follow a specific course of training and undergo a series of assessments to confirm that you’ve reached the required standard. * The apprenticeship consists of 7 phases: 3 off\-the\-job and 4 on\-the\-job. SOLAS and the Department of Education and Science have responsibility for providing the off\-the\-job training, whilst your on\-the\-job training will take place on site with Mercury. While attending off the job training, the apprentice will be required to complete a portfolio \& series of exams, both theory \& practical * All apprentice salary rates are in line with the electrical \& mechanical sectoral employment orders. **Apprentice Plus Program** The Apprentice Plus Program has been developed by Mercury and the program runs alongside your apprenticeship, opening the gateway to future management and leadership. **Essential Criteria:** * Must have a valid safe pass card. * Must have a valid manual handling certificate. * Must be 17 years of age or over. * Must have an up\-to\-date apprenticeship logbook. * Subject to interview and reference checks. **All applications for apprenticeships must be submitted with the mandatory requirements listed below**. **Interviews will not be considered without these requirements.** **Mandatory Requirements:** * Valid Safe Pass * Valid Manual Handling * Up to date CV * Results of Phase 2, Phase 4 \& Phase 6 (if applicable) * 2 x Reference Contacts * Proof of eligibility to work in Ireland. ***Subject to interview and reference checks*** ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.***

title: ADVICE ASSOCIATE (URGENT - FINANCIAL ADVICE EXPERIENCE )
description: ADVICE ASSOCIATE \- FINANCIAL ADVICE INDUSTRY (APPLY IF WITH EXPERIENCE) Location: Remote Type: Full\-time Salary: AUD$10\-$15ph **About Life Unshackled** LifeUnshackled is a platform built to help people reclaim the life they once dreamed of, clients and team alike. We exist because “retire comfortably” is not the goal, freedom is. We bring bold financial strategy and strong follow\-through so clients can build real wealth and live life on their terms. You will be joining a growth\-minded team that cares about ambition, high standards, and doing work that genuinely helps people unshackle their lives. **About The Role** The Associate is the operational and relational lead for their assigned clients, acting as the Chief Operating Officer of each client relationship. This role combines precision in data and document management, project management discipline, people and communication skills, hands\-on platform execution, and a delegation mindset. Each Associate is responsible for ensuring timely, compliant, and high\-quality delivery of financial advice implementation and client outcomes, in coordination with the broader LifeUnshackled team. **Four Key Areas Of Responsibilities** * The Information Steward * Custodian of all essential client information and access rights. * Curate and maintain a “Client Support Pack” with critical data and supporting documents for rapid use in preparing application forms. This includes identity documents (individuals and entities), FATCA, ABNs, ACNs, TFNs, ESAs, bank accounts, wholesale certificates, etc. * Collect and store tax\-related and performance\-related documentation (e.g. holding, distribution, loan, rental, annual, market value, tax statements). * Maintain and enforce the standard client folder structure, file naming conventions, and financial year categorisation so that files are audit\-ready at all times. * Own the client Fact Find end\-to\-end — keeping it current after every meeting, statement, or client communication, and ensuring it underpins all advice documents as an auditable chain of evidence. * Monitor and update the real\-time client balance sheet, sourcing data from various platforms and reconciling across systems quarterly. * Ensure adviser access and login credentials are in place and securely managed across all relevant platforms. * Collaborate with the PSM to standardise and streamline information management. * Mindset: “Would I be confident this is clean and client\-ready at any time?” * The Delivery Project Manager * Leader of accurate client service implementation and delivery timelines. * Ensure investment transactions are implemented same\-day and insurance applications are begun within 48 hours of approval. * Turn meeting actions and strategic goals into clearly written, trackable tasks — nothing falls through the cracks. * Own timelines and deadlines — chasing overdue tasks, flagging bottlenecks, and ensuring every task reaches a clear resolution. * Delegate technical or administrative tasks appropriately while owning the outcome. * Coordinate closely with paraplanners, the Practice Support Manager, the Principal Adviser, and external professionals. * Ensure documents and forms are completed correctly, on time, and sent to the right parties. * Mindset: “What’s needed to move this forward without needing Principal involvement?” * The Relationship Anchor * Point of continuity, context, and connection for each client. * Understand the needs and preferences of your clients and orchestrate effective service delivery accordingly. * Be the primary internal advocate and coordinator for your clients. * Track developments in the client’s personal and financial life. * Stay across all meeting notes and ensure task systems reflect meetings. * Notice changes in goals, mindset, or relationships and raise them with the Principal. * Support the Principal by ensuring they are well\-informed before every meeting. * Ensure the client never has to repeat themselves or chase us for updates. * Mindset: “What’s shifted for the client that we haven’t yet actioned?” * The Compliant Storyteller * Ensuring each client file tells a compelling and compliant story. * Keep client files audit\-ready with consistent, clear, and updated notes. * Manage the full post\-meeting workflow: consent and transcription, file note generation and review, follow\-up email s, and updating the Fact Find with any new information. * Ensure fact finds, SOAs, ROAs, and client communications are accurate and well\-organised. * Maintain clarity in the chronology and context of advice decisions. * Highlight progress and transformation in the client’s journey, tying actions back to their goals. * Ensure documentation not only meets compliance and privacy standards but tells the narrative of value delivered. * Mindset: “Would this file make sense to an outsider, showing a clear and compliant story of impact?” PRIORITISATION ORDER * From a business perspective, your tasks should be prioritised in the following order: * Cash / investment transacting, including opening of associated accounts and completion of any KYC / onboarding requirements with third parties where this would delay a cash / investment transaction. * Insurance applications and getting policies into force. * Preparing meeting summaries and post\-meeting email s for your clients, and any sub\-10\-minute tasks that are directly requested of you by the Principal Adviser. * Responding to requests made of us by other professionals (we expect fast responses from them and should return the favour). * Other work pertaining to clients who are in their first 6 weeks with us (including onboarding). * Other work pertaining to your broader client list, prioritised by the annual fee that they are paying us and any relationship considerations (including preparing their reports / maintaining their client file with current information / supporting loan applications / etc). * Balance this priority list with approaching deadlines — as deadlines near, the work becomes a higher priority. * Anything you can’t get to in a reasonable time, just flag it, and we can try to delegate to others or make a business decision to defer that task. REPORTING \& COLLABORATION Reports to: Practice Support Manager (PSM) for delivery oversight; Principal Adviser for client outcomes. Collaborates with: PSM, Paraplanner, Admin Assistant, Principal Adviser. Indicators of Success * 100% of clients have complete, current documentation and adviser access. * Tasks are delivered on time with minimal Principal intervention. * High service ratings and positive feedback from assigned clients. * Accurate and timely handoff of technical and administrative work. * Adviser’s administrative workload is reduced for each client in your portfolio. * Investment transactions executed same\-day; insurance applications commenced within 48 hours. * Demonstrated proficiency across all required platforms. Expected Behaviour * Proactive, reliable, and accountable. * Thoughtful and detail\-oriented. * Able to prioritise and escalate as needed. * Collaborative, not siloed. * Focused on both client satisfaction and internal efficiency. APPLICATION PROCESS **To Apply, Please Submit The Following** * A 2–5 minute video introducing yourself and explaining why you are the best fit for this role * Your updated CV and LinkedIn profile link * Names and contact details of three references (2 professional \+ 1 personal) Please apply and email all details to: \-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\- ONLY those who follow the instructions in how to apply will be accommodated.

title: AI Systems Engineering Instructor
description: 🤓At TripleTen, we're building an international platform of digital re\-skilling products to help people from diverse backgrounds learn new professions and build sustainable careers in tech. *Our program graduates have landed jobs at top companies like Tesla, Spotify, PlayStation, Google, and Microsoft. So far, over 5,000 students have chosen TripleTen, and hundreds of new students are joining us each month. We believe everyone can jumpstart a new career, which is why we produce quality educational content and offer intensive courses in tech complete with full access to an interactive platform, code reviews, and support from industry practitioners.* We're launching a program for experienced engineers transitioning into system architecture roles. The curriculum is being built by a team of senior authors. What we need now is the person who brings it to life for students: hosts the live sessions, reviews the work, runs the architecture review boards, and sets the technical bar for the people supporting alongside you. **This is a teaching and review role.** You'll be the senior technical presence the cohort learns from week to week. You'll run live sessions and office hours, give real architectural feedback on student deliverables, and act as the escalation point for a team of supporting instructors who handle first\-line questions. The best person for this is someone who has actually designed and shipped systems at scale, has opinions about what good architecture looks like, and can tell an experienced engineer *why* their design is wrong, not just whether it runs. **Your audience:** experienced engineers leveling up to system architecture roles. Many already write production code daily. They will spot shallow feedback instantly, so the bar here is real seniority, not familiarity. **Format:** Async\-first with scheduled live sessions. Live sessions and office hours are timed around a US\-based cohort. Estimated 15 to 25 hrs/week. **Please submit all resumes or CV's in English.** What you will do: * Host weekly live sessions across the program, focused on the design and decision layer: where to split services, how systems scale and fail, which tradeoff to make and why * Run group office hours and 1:1 sessions for students working through projects and the capstone * Review student projects against rubrics: the architecture dossiers, deployed backends, cloud infrastructure, performance reports, security reviews, and the final distributed\-system capstone * Give architectural feedback, not just pass/fail checks. Catch when a design decision is unsound, an interface is wrong, or a scope is unrealistic, and explain the better path * Run the mock architecture review boards and the simulated executive\-panel presentations, and give structured, senior\-level critique * Set the technical standard for a team of supporting instructors who cover questions and first\-line review, and act as their escalation point on design and architecture calls Requirements: * 5\+ years as a senior software/systems architect, platform engineer, or senior backend/infrastructure engineer * Has designed and shipped systems at scale in a real company, not just coursework or side projects * Depth across the architectural spine: system design, API and service architecture, cloud and infrastructure (AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform, CI/CD), and distributed systems * Can explain why a decision was made, not just how it was implemented, and can diagnose and critique someone else's architecture live * Strong technical communication: comfortable leading a live session and writing clear, specific review feedback * Strong English C1\+ — instruction and review are in English for a US\-based audience * Comfortable using AI tools in day\-to\-day technical work What we can offer you: * Milestone\-based payments tied to deliverables * Fully remote work with flexible hours * A digital office — we use modern tools (Notion, Miro, Slack, Figma) to keep collaboration smooth * Professional trust and autonomy — no micromanaging * The chance to see real impact: our metric is graduates landing jobs in tech * Join a diverse, international, and close\-knit team excited to work with you

title: AI/ML Agent Engineer — Front-End Focus
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. ****Role Overview**** We’re hiring a Junior AI/ML Agent Engineer with a front\-end focus to build agentic workflows and tools that integrate LLMs, OpenAI specs, MCP, and modern frontend frameworks. You’ll collaborate with product and platform teams to deliver intuitive web experiences that harness AI agents for developer and business workflows. ****Key Responsibilities**** * Design and implement agentic workflows demonstrating LLMs (Claude, OpenAI), MCP clients/servers, and OpenAI specifications for tool/function orchestration. * Build user\-facing features in AngularJS and ReactJS, with services and tooling in Node.js and Python for agent integration. * Implement prompt engineering, tool schemas, and function calling patterns; handle context windows, memory, and retrieval strategies for agent reliability. * Integrate agents with developer ecosystems: GitHub (actions, PR comment bots), Cline (AI coding assistant workflows), and Claude model endpoints. * Develop reusable UI components and SDKs for agent invocation, status visualization, and error handling; contribute to design systems and accessibility. * Ship high\-quality code with unit/e2e tests, CI/CD, and observability (logs, metrics, traces) for agent runs and UI flows. * Document architecture, OpenAI/MCP spec contracts, and developer guides for internal consumers. **Minimum Qualifications** * 2–5 years of professional experience in software engineering with front\-end emphasis. * Hands\-on experience building with LLMs (OpenAI, Claude) and agent/workflow patterns (tools, function calling, retries, guardrails). * Working knowledge of MCP (Model Context Protocol) concepts and implementation of clients/servers. * Proficient in AngularJS and/or ReactJS, plus Node.js and Python for agent backends and tooling. * Familiarity with OpenAI specifications (tool definitions, schemas) and API integration best practices. * Experience integrating with GitHub (Actions, APIs), and exposure to Cline and Claude models and strong fundamentals: TypeScript/JavaScript, REST/GraphQL, testing (Jest/Cypress), linting, and accessibility. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with RAG pipelines, embeddings, or lightweight vector stores for context augmentation. * Basic security awareness for agent design: input validation, secrets management, rate limiting, and least\-privilege tokens. * Observability with Datadog/OpenTelemetry or similar for agent runs and UI interactions. * Knowledge of prompt versioning, evaluation (e.g., golden sets), and experiment design. ****What We Value**** * Eagerness to learn and adapt to emerging models/agent frameworks. * Bias for action, pragmatic problem\-solving, and ownership of outcomes. * Clear communication and collaborative mindset across product, design, and infra. ****Salary \& Benefits**** Competitive mid\-level compensation with benefits. Final level and pay depend on experience and interview performance. How to Apply Submit your resume, GitHub/portfolio links, and a brief note describing an agent or AI\-enabled feature you’ve built, including frameworks, models, and evaluation approach **.** **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: AI/ML Engineer
description: Our client is seeking an experienced AI/ML Engineer specialising in Artificial Intelligence and Large Language Models to innovate and strengthen their pioneering security platforms. This role focuses on building and deploying intelligent tools using leading AI/LLM technologies for automated red\-teaming, vulnerability detection, adversarial attack simulation, and intelligent defence mechanisms. You'll work hands\-on to operationalise AI models, contributing to secure and robust model deployments. You'll collaborate closely with AI researchers, software engineers, and product teams to productionise cutting\-edge AI solutions for securing and optimising AI\-powered systems and processes. **AI/ML Model Development \& Application** * Research, design, develop, fine\-tune, and evaluate LLMs, intelligent agents, and deep learning models for AI security and system optimisation * Create advanced tools for automated red\-teaming, vulnerability detection, adversarial attack simulation, and intelligent defences * Implement and deploy AI/LLM\-powered features as robust, scalable, and secure components within the client's security platforms * Optimise AI/LLM models for inference speed, cost\-efficiency, and resource utilisation in production **MLOps \& AI Workflow Pipeline Development** * Develop and maintain pipelines for end\-to\-end AI/LLM workflows covering data preprocessing, training, fine\-tuning, validation, deployment, and monitoring * Proactively monitor model performance and behaviour in production, addressing safety, alignment, and security vulnerabilities * Apply software engineering best practices to AI/LLM workflows for secure and seamless deployments **Collaborative Development \& Innovation** * Partner with cross\-functional teams, including researchers and engineers, to productionise cutting\-edge AI/LLM techniques and integrate them into the client's products * Translate technical advancements into practical solutions aligned with customer needs and business objectives * Communicate findings and solutions through documentation and presentations for data\-driven decision\-making **AI Security \& Emerging Research** * Develop tools and strategies to detect and mitigate vulnerabilities such as prompt injection and data poisoning threats * Investigate and utilise advancements in Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to improve AI model reliability and alignment * Continuously stay updated on industry trends and research to integrate state\-of\-the\-art AI/LLM advancements into the platform **Requirements** **Required Qualifications** * Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field * 3\-5 years of professional experience, including hands\-on experience developing, training, and deploying AI/LLM models * Familiarity with AI/LLM libraries and tools such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, Hugging Face Transformers, or similar frameworks * Understanding of LLM security vulnerabilities and implementing mitigation solutions * Proven expertise in deploying AI/ML models in production environments, with emphasis on secure deployments * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills applied to complex AI challenges **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience fine\-tuning and deploying large\-scale language models, including using frameworks like vLLM or TGI * Familiarity with Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) for improving LLM alignment and performance * Knowledge of vector databases (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate) and their applications in AI workflows * Expertise with cloud platforms and distributed computing for large\-scale AI operations * Hands\-on experience optimising models for cost, speed, and resource efficiency in production workflows **What Success Looks Like** * Demonstrating leadership in delivering robust, scalable, and secure AI/LLM\-powered solutions * Collaborating effectively with cross\-functional teams to align technical solutions with business needs and customer priorities * Developing innovative tools and methods to identify and mitigate security vulnerabilities within AI systems * Consistently applying advanced analytical techniques and operationalising data\-driven insights to enhance platform efficiency and reliability **Benefits** * Comprehensive Private Medical Coverage * Support for Mental Health Expenses * Life Insurance Options * Attractive Compensation Package **About C\-Serv** C\-Serv is a global IT consulting and workforce solutions firm operating across 50\+ countries. We build high\-performing teams for technology\-led organisations, specialising in cybersecurity, cloud, AI, networking, and software engineering. We're 100% woman\-owned, and our values — Empathy, Integrity, Collaboration, and Growth — govern how we hire, work, and treat every person we engage with. **Diversity \& Inclusion** We champion diversity in every hire and are committed to equal opportunity regardless of race, religion, colour, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, marital status, or veteran status.

title: Administrative Coordinator
description: The **Institute of Public Administration (IPA)** is seeking an experienced and highly organised **Administrative Co\-Ordinator** to join the Professional Development Department, supporting the Business Development and Leadership and Management teams. This Grade 5 role will play a key part in ensuring the smooth delivery of the IPA’s learning and development programmes, providing high\-quality administrative, operational, and customer service support across a busy and fast\-paced environment. The successful candidate will be responsible for delivering end\-to\-end administrative support for a range of in\-person, blended, and online training programmes and events. This will include coordination of bookings, invoicing, scheduling, logistics, course materials, learner access on the LMS, and support for marketing and communications activity. The role is central to ensuring a seamless and professional experience for clients and participants across all IPA programmes. Working closely with colleagues across the Professional Development Department, the Administrative Co\-Ordinator will support the efficient operation of systems, processes, and procedures, ensuring accuracy, consistency, and compliance in all administrative activities. * Strong interpersonal skills and commitment to delivering excellent customer service. * Ability to deal with difficult situations with tact, discretion and professionalism. * Strong analytical and problem solving skills. * Proven ability to work on own initiative and work in a demanding, high volume and flexible team environment \- *essential* * Willing to work in a team and build trust with colleagues. * Commitment to continuous professional development. * Ability to adapt to changing requirements. * Flexible approach to working hours as the demands of the post may occasionally require work outside normal office working hours. * Each candidate shall be in a state of health such as would indicate a reasonable prospect of ability to render regular and efficient service. **How to title: Apple Retail Service Technician
description: **Retail Service Technician** **King St** **Full\-time** **€30,000 per annum plus performance related bonus** **Have you ever wondered what is inside your iPhone or how it works?** Select (part of the Select Technology Group) is the leading Apple Premium Reseller in the UK and Ireland with stores across the UK and Ireland. We are looking for a dynamic individual with the natural drive and enthusiasm to become dedicated Retail Service Technician in our store in King St. This is an exciting role where no two days are ever the same. You will receive full on the job training to help you make a success of this fantastic opportunity. Within 6 months of joining us, we would anticipate that you will have achieved the recognised accreditation of Apple Certified iPhone Technician (ACiT). Working as part of a supportive and knowledgeable team, the Retail Service Technician is at the heart of the overall performance of your store with regards to service and repairs. We will provide all the training and support you need for you to establish your store as a consistent, efficient and high performing store. You will be responsible for maintaining the high standards required within an Apple Premium Reseller and ensuring that customers are delighted with the repair and support services we provide. As you progress within the role, we will provide all the development you need to be able to undertake all aspects of servicerelated duties carried out in store including initial troubleshooting, booking devices in for service, physical repair of iOS devices, inventory management, customer relations and control over Apple metric scores. Showcasing the full Apple product line in a customer friendly environment, Select is renowned for providing outstanding customer service. The training you will receive will give you the enviable position of being the first to hear about new Apple products and initiatives. You will be joining a team which is extremely driven to not only meet but exceed all sales and service performance goals, all whilst demonstrating your personal ethos of 'no limits'. Your store will provide our customers with the best impression of our business, attaining where possible the required metrics set out by Apple. What do you get from working at Select? * A growing love and passion for all things Apple * Competitive salary * Pension Scheme (subject to eligibility) * Fantastic Employee discounts * Employee Incentives * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) * Recognition of Long Service If this sounds like a place you would like to work, you are aged 18 years or over, have the Right To Work in Ireland and you can demonstrate your passion, knowledge and determination, then we would like to hear from you. Please note that this role is a full\-time role. To apply for the role of Retail Service Technician, please submit your CV. **IND1**

title: Apprentice Electrician
description: **Company Description** Silent\-Aire, founded in 1994, is a technology\-driven company that designs, engineers, manufactures, and services hyperscale data center solutions. The company’s portfolio includes modular data centers, power room modules, turnkey modular deployments, custom HVAC systems, and mission\-critical services. Silent\-Aire employs over 1,000 team members across seven manufacturing facilities totaling 650,000 sq/ft in Edmonton, Alberta, Gilbert, Arizona, and Dublin, Ireland, with additional sales and service centers in the United States and Ireland. The company’s innovative solutions are trusted by leading hyperscale data center owners and operators. Silent\-Aire offers a dynamic work environment focused on technical excellence, reliability, and continuous improvement. **Role Description** This is a full\-time, on\-site Apprentice Electrician role based in Dublin. The Apprentice Electrician will support licensed electricians with installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical systems and equipment used in hyperscale data center solutions and related manufacturing operations. Day\-to\-day responsibilities include assisting with wiring, conduit installation, panel work, terminations, and equipment hookup under appropriate supervision, while following electrical codes, drawings, and specifications. The role involves performing basic testing and troubleshooting, organizing tools and materials, and helping to maintain a safe, clean, and compliant work environment. The Apprentice Electrician will also participate in training, toolbox talks, and mentorship activities to develop technical skills and progress through the apprenticeship program. **Qualifications** * Foundational knowledge of electricity and safe electrical practices, with a strong interest in developing technical expertise. * Ability to support general electrical work, including assisting with installations, wiring, and equipment setup under supervision. * Skills or aptitude in maintenance \& repair and troubleshooting of electrical systems and components. * Interest in applying basic electrical engineering principles to practical, hands\-on work in a data center and manufacturing environment. * Enrollment in, or eligibility for, an electrician apprenticeship program or equivalent vocational/technical training is preferred. * Ability to read and follow technical drawings, schematics, and work instructions, with strong attention to detail and safety. * Good communication and teamwork skills, with the ability to take direction and work effectively in a fast\-paced, industrial setting. * Physical ability to perform manual tasks, including standing, bending, lifting, and working with tools and equipment for extended periods.

title: Assoc Dir, Drug Product Development
description: **Job Details** --------------- Req ID: R011248 Locations: Dublin, IE; London, London, City of, UK; California \- \[**Remote/Home\-Based**] Category: Technical Operations **If you are a current Jazz employee please apply via the Internal Career site.** Jazz Pharmaceuticals is a global biopharma company whose purpose is to innovate to transform the lives of patients and their families. We are dedicated to developing life\-changing medicines for people with serious diseases — often with limited or no therapeutic options. We have a diverse portfolio of marketed medicines, including leading therapies for sleep disorders and epilepsy, and a growing portfolio of cancer treatments. Our patient\-focused and science\-driven approach powers pioneering research and development advancements across our robust pipeline of innovative therapeutics in oncology and neuroscience. Jazz is headquartered in Dublin, Ireland with research and development laboratories, manufacturing facilities and employees in multiple countries committed to serving patients worldwide. Please visit www.jazzpharmaceuticals.com for more information. The Associate Director of Drug Product Development is responsible for providing scientific and technical leadership for Jazz Pharmaceuticals’ Pharmaceutical Development programs at various stages of the development life\-cycle including preformulation, formulation and process development, technical transfer and validation. The Associate Director of Drug Product Development will oversee drug product aspects of development and manufacturing projects from pre\-formulation and product feasibility through to clinical trial supply and technology transfer/commercialization. In addition, the Associate Director of Drug Product Development may provide leadership of cross\-functional CMC development teams. The Associate Director of Drug Product Development will have broad experience in pharmaceutical product development, demonstrated problem solving ability, module 3 authorship, development of robust manufacturing processes through designed experiments and data analysis, and excellent project management, teamwork and leadership skills. **Essential** **Functions** * Leads the development of small molecule drug product formulations, appropriate to the phase of pharmaceutical development, with sufficient stability and robustness for clinical and/or commercial manufacturing. * Responsible for specifying product and process requirements, including manufacturing equipment and methods, performance criteria, materials, test protocols, required analytical methodology, packaging (clinical and commercial); and in developing robust processes for pharmaceutical production. * Directs projects and leads cross\-functional teams (as required) with technical proficiency, scientific creativity, collaboration with others to achieve project goals on time and within budget. * Create intellectual property covering Jazz Pharmaceuticals products and ensure freedom to operate in the development of new drug products * Support the identification and selection of suitable drug product development and commercial manufacturing vendors (CMOs). * Direct technical projects (internally and at CDMOs) including developing detailed technical work plans and managing performance of vendors. * Work cooperatively with, or lead, internal and external teams as required. * Author CMC sections of regulatory dossiers for products at all stages to enable acceptance by global regulators. * Supports asset due diligence and new product introduction and integrations * current knowledge of cGMP and industry standards for process design and validation to manage vendors and contract development organizations. **Required Knowledge, Skills, and** **Abilities** * \>10 years’ experience in drug product development in the pharmaceutical industry required. Direct experience with formulation development, process development, technical transfer and/or process validation. * Demonstrated technical proficiency, engineering, collaboration with others, and independent thought. Strong teamwork skills. * Demonstrated troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills including the use of designed experiments, statistical process control, * Development/authorship of CMC regulatory filings for pharmaceutical products. * Broad exposure to multiple drug delivery technologies and knowledge of standard dosage forms preferred. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Current knowledge of quality systems and FDA and EU regulations as they relate to pharmaceutical development and validation. * Experience in intellectual property development desirable. * Proven project management skills for technical programs. * Flexibility to travel on company business when required **Required/Preferred Education and Licenses** * Advanced degree in Chemistry, Pharm. Sci., Engineering, or related field, or equivalent combination of education and work\-related experience required. **Description of Physical Demands** * Occasional mobility within office environment. Routinely sitting for extended periods of time. * Constantly operating a computer, printer, telephone and other similar office machinery. *Jazz Pharmaceuticals is an equal opportunity/affirmative action employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, disability status, protected veteran status, or any characteristic protected by law.* **FOR US BASED CANDIDATES ONLY** Jazz Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is committed to fair and equitable compensation practices and we strive to provide employees with total compensation packages that are market competitive. For this role, the full and complete base pay range is: $157,600\.00 \- $236,400\.00 Individual compensation paid within this range will depend on many factors, including qualifications, skills, relevant experience, job knowledge, and other pertinent factors. The goal is to ensure fair and competitive compensation aligned with the candidate's expertise and contributions, within the established pay framework and our Total Compensation philosophy. Internal equity considerations will also influence individual base pay decisions. This range will be reviewed on a regular basis. At Jazz, your base pay is only one part of your total compensation package. The successful candidate may also be eligible for a discretionary annual cash bonus or incentive compensation (depending on the role), in accordance with the terms of the Company's Global Cash Bonus Plan or Incentive Compensation Plan, as well as discretionary equity grants in accordance with Jazz's Long Term Equity Incentive Plan. The successful candidate will also be eligible to participate in various benefits offerings, including, but not limited to, medical, dental and vision insurance, 401k retirement savings plan, and flexible paid vacation. For more information on our Benefits offerings : https://careers.jazzpharma.com/benefits.html.

title: Associate Professor in Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (Social Media Analytics)
description: This Job is based in Australia Associate Professor in Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing (Social Media Analytics) Apply now **Job no:** 540051 **Work type:** Full Time **Location:** Sydney, NSW **Categories:** Associate Professor **Job Details** * Salary, Level D – AUD $185,917 to $204,218 per annum \+ 17% superannuation * Full\-time * 2\-Year Convertible Tenure Track or continuing appointment * Location: Kensington – Sydney, Australia **The Opportunity** Join the School of Computer Science and Engineering in the Faculty of Engineering at UNSW as an Associate Professor in a Teaching and Research role. As an Associate Professor, you will be a senior academic leader at one of Australia’s premier computer science schools, contributing to and shaping the strategic direction of research and education in Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing. You will drive high\-impact research with national and international visibility across areas such as large language models, social media analytics, and the application of AI and NLP to understanding and improving online information ecosystems, including social media platforms and digital communication environments, while playing a key role in advancing the School’s research profile, industry engagement, and educational excellence. With established strengths in machine learning, large language models, data science, cybersecurity, and distributed systems, UNSW offers an exceptional environment for ambitious academics who are ready to lead innovative programs, mentor emerging researchers, and contribute to institutional strategy, governance, and interdisciplinary collaboration. You will help advance the future of intelligent, trustworthy, and responsible AI systems. This is an opportunity to join a global leader in Computer Science, with cutting\-edge facilities, world\-class faculty, and an innovative research environment. With strong industry partnerships and a commitment to sustainability, UNSW provides unparalleled opportunities for leadership, collaboration, and impact. **About You** You are an established academic with a strong and sustained track record of high\-impact research in Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing, and a growing national or international reputation in your field. You bring expertise in areas such as large language models, social media analytics, model alignment, and interpretability, with an interest in applying these to real\-world challenges in online information ecosystems and demonstrate the ability to translate research into real\-world impact. With a proven ability to secure competitive research funding, lead research initiatives, and supervise higher degree research students to successful completion, you are also an engaging and effective educator, with experience delivering high\-quality teaching and contributing to curriculum development. As a senior academic, you will demonstrate clear leadership within your discipline, including contributing to strategic initiatives, academic governance, or program development. You are collaborative, forward\-thinking, and motivated to build strong partnerships across academia and industry. **About UNSW** UNSW is a world‑leading institution recognised for its scale, prestige, and impact. With strong industry engagement and partnerships across sectors, UNSW provides a unique environment where academic expertise translates into real‑world outcomes. The university is home to cutting‑edge research that drives innovation and societal progress, while its excellence in teaching ensures students are prepared to lead in their fields. title: BIM Detailer
description: **BIM Detailer/Technician** Leixlip, Co. Kildare. **Company Description:** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description:** As a BIM Detailer/Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in our project development and implementation process. You will work closely with the Project Team Leads as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers, Construction team and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. Current role is based in Leixleip, Co. Kildare. **Role and Responsibilities will include:** * Model Development: Create detailed 3D BIM models of mechanical systems, including HVAC, plumbing, and process via P\&ID schematics, using an array of Autodesk Products along with the latest Point Cloud Technology and full support methodology, ensuring accuracy and completeness. * Co\-ordination: Work within and experienced BIM team liaising with architects, structural engineers, and other project stakeholders to resolve design conflicts and ensure seamless integration of all systems within the BIM model. * Clash Detection: Utilise software like Navisworks Manage to identify and resolve clashes between mechanical systems and other building components before construction. * Documentation: Prepare 3D models to produce 2D design drawings, shop drawings, based on the accurate and fully constructable BIM models. * BIM Standards \& Compliance: Ensure that all modelling activities adhere to project\-specific BIM requirements, industry standards, and the project's BIM Execution Plan (BEP). * Technical Support: Provide technical support to junior members of the project team, assisting with the implementation and use of BIM technology along with attendance at required meetings. * Revisions \& Optimization: Manage revision and change to the models and conduct design analysis to optimize system performance and identify technical challenges. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. **Skills and Requirements:** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Technical Proficiency: Strong proficiency in BIM software, in particular AutoCAD, Plant 3D and Navisworks. * Mechanical Systems Knowledge: A solid understanding of mechanical engineering principles and systems, such as HVAC and piping. * Collaboration Skills: Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * Problem\-Solving Abilities: Strong problem\-solving skills to identify and resolve technical challenges and design conflicts. * BIM Workflow Understanding: Knowledge of BIM workflows, content standards, and the ability to apply them to projects * Verbal and Written Communication: The ability to clearly explain complex technical information to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders is vital for project success. * Teamwork: Working effectively with diverse teams, including architects, structural engineers, and project managers, is fundamental in a collaborative BIM environment. * Interpersonal Skills: Strong interpersonal skills, including active listening and constructive conflict resolution, help foster positive working relationships. * Problem\-Solving \& Analysis: The ability to identify, analyse, and resolve complex issues within BIM models, especially those related to clashes and integration, is critical. * Attention to Detail: High precision in modelling, documentation, and coordination tasks prevents errors and ensures the quality of the final output. * Adaptability and Flexibility: The construction and BIM industries evolve rapidly, so engineers must be flexible to new technologies and fast\-paced environments. * Continuous Learning: A commitment to ongoing learning is essential to keep up with advanced BIM tools, software updates, and new methodologies. * Organization and Time Management: Effectively managing tasks and meeting project deadlines is crucial for delivering projects on time. * Project Management Support: Experience in supporting project management activities, including scheduling and cost estimation, is beneficial. **Interested parties should apply here and/or mail colinodonnell@joneseng.com** **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note. We are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time.

title: Bioprocess Associate
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Overview:** Client will provide Biologics Drug Substance manufacturing for Clinical Supply, Registration \& Commercial Launch. The facility serves as a magnetic force that attracts, recognizes, and integrates people of diverse backgrounds and perspectives. **Requirements** **Role Functions:** * The Manufacturing Bioprocess Associate will support end\-to end production operations for both Fed Batch and Continuous Manufacturing. **Primary activities/responsibilities:** * Operate equipment according to electronic batch records, sampling plans and standard operating procedures. * Work as part of a multi\-disciplined, diverse and dedicated process team where flexibility and teamwork are an essential requirement * Support weekend or out of hours work on select time frames to support manufacturing activities. * Conduct all work activities with a strict adherence to the safety and compliance culture on site * Support the Authoring of electronic batch records, sampling plans, work instructions and standard operating procedures. **Required Education, Experience and Skills:** * Level 6 qualification in a science or engineering discipline desired. * 2\+ years experience in a GMP Manufacturing requirement shall be deemed equivalent * Ability to work independently and within a cross\-functional team. * Familiarity with contamination control and batch release requirements * Familiarity with Emerson DeltaV, Pas X, and the use of Automation in a manufacturing Process. * Experience with various SingleUse technologies in a manufacturing environment **Preferred Experience and Skills:** * Understanding of Upstream and Downstream Unit Operations for mAb manufacturing **Reports to:** Drug Substance Process Operations Manager

title: Building Management Systems Engineer
description: **About the Role** This role involves delivering high\-quality after\-sales support and field service activities across a range of BMS and HVAC control systems throughout European projects and client sites. The successful candidate will be responsible for fault finding, diagnostics, reactive callouts, system optimisation, repairs, maintenance and technical support activities while ensuring minimal disruption to client operations. The role requires strong knowledge of Building Management Systems including expertise in one or more of the following platforms: Trend, Distech, Tridium/Niagara, Siemens or other industry\-standard systems. The engineer will work closely with clients and internal teams to troubleshoot issues, improve system performance and maintain reliable operation across Commercial, Retail, Pharmaceutical and Data Centre environments. Occasional commissioning and small upgrade works may also form part of the role where required. **Key Responsibilities** **Safety and Compliance** * Follow company safety procedures, RAMS, permits and client site rules at all times. * Participate in site inductions, toolbox talks and safety briefings as required. * Apply Stop Work Authority when conditions are unsafe or unclear. * Ensure all service activities are carried out safely and professionally within live operational environments. * Report hazards, incidents and near misses promptly. **Service \& Technical Support** * Attend reactive service callouts and diagnose BMS, controls and HVAC\-related faults. * Carry out fault finding on controllers, field devices, sensors, actuators, panels and communication networks. * Repair, replace or reconfigure defective components and restore systems to operational status. * Investigate intermittent issues and provide practical long\-term solutions. * Support planned preventative maintenance (PPM) activities and system health checks. * Optimise control strategies, alarms, schedules, setpoints and graphics to improve system performance and energy efficiency. * Provide technical support to clients both remotely and on\-site. * Assist with small retrofit, upgrade and modification works where required. * Carry out minor commissioning and recommissioning activities following repairs or upgrades. **BMS Platforms \& Integration** * Configure, troubleshoot and maintain BMS platforms and front\-end systems. * Diagnose BACnet/Modbus integrations (TCP/RTU), network issues and communication faults. * Support integration of third\-party systems and devices. * Maintain backups and ensure system databases are updated correctly following service interventions. **Customer Support \& Communication** * Develop strong working relationships with clients and provide professional after\-sales support. * Clearly communicate technical issues, findings and recommended corrective actions to clients and internal teams. * Respond to urgent client requirements in a timely and professional manner. * Provide service reports and recommendations following site visits. **Quality, Documentation \& Reporting** * Complete accurate service reports, fault logs, issue trackers and maintenance documentation. * Maintain detailed records of system changes, backups and remedial works completed. * Ensure all documentation is uploaded to the appropriate company systems in line with internal procedures. * Ensure work areas are left safe, tidy and professionally presented following completion of works. **Collaboration \& Development** * Coordinate with operations, projects, design teams and subcontractors to resolve technical issues. * Support apprentices and junior engineers through mentoring and knowledge sharing. * Contribute to continuous improvement of service processes, templates and client support standards. *The above list of responsibilities is not exhaustive and may be subject to change in line with business needs* **Qualification/Skills/Competencies/Experience** * 3–5 years’ experience in BMS service with an electrical background, maintenance, troubleshooting or commissioning environments. * Qualified Electrician with strong fault\-finding and diagnostic capability across BMS and HVAC systems. * Experience with either Niagara N4/Tridium, Trend, Siemens, and Allen Bradley, good knowledge of Modbus and BACnet preferred. * Good understanding of HVAC plant operation and control strategies. * Strong customer\-facing communication and problem\-solving skills. * High standard of safety awareness, documentation discipline and professionalism. * Full driving licence and willingness to travel/work flexible hours where required.

leadership levels * Commercially astute, with the ability to contribute to pricing strategies, margin evaluation, and contract risk assessment * Highly self\-motivated, with the ability to work independently while also contributing effectively within a fast\-paced senior team * Full clean driving licence and willingness to travel across Ireland and the UK as required Preferred * Established network within Irish and/or UK utility providers, renewable developers, DNO/TSO organisations, data centre ecosystem, or Tier 1 contractors * Experience managing or coordinating tender submissions with multidisciplinary bid teams * Degree in engineering, business, or a related discipline (though proven results are prioritised) * Strong knowledge of infrastructure investment strategies across **What You Will Receive:** * Base Salary: €75,000 \- €90,000\+ (depending on experience) * Bonus: Up to 20% * Vehicle: Company vehicle or car allowance * Pension: Employer contribution scheme * Annual solid reputation within telecoms and utilities, supported by a skilled workforce and an ambitious leadership team.
