You will be part of the management team in an interesting and diverse role helping to drive improvements across all departments in a very progressive group. **Role \& Responsibilities:** * Ensure continued compliance with all relevant waste, transport licences and permits * Ensure continued compliance with all relevant health \& safety legislation * Conduct on\-site sampling, monitoring and reporting in accordance with licence conditions * Keep up to date with all new and upcoming relevant legislation * Maintain accurate records and prepare reports, letters, and memorandums with respect to compliance matters including monthly and annual returns to EPA, TFS, CSO, County Councils, REPAK * Management of the 3 ISO certifications, Environment, Health \& Safety and Quality driving continual improvement of each. * Continuous training of employees in the procedures and systems in conjunction with HR and other department managers. * Liaise with members of the public, professional bodies, customers and suppliers on compliance \& legal related issues and training of customers in good waste management practices * Conducting site audits and taking responsibility to ensure all issues are addressed and closed out appropriately with the relevant managers. * Create and manage customer waste reports including system checking of the accuracy of the weighing information and working with other departments to continually improve the systems and reporting functionality * Assist business development with proposals \& tenders as required. **Skills \& Qualifications:** * Qualified environmental or health \& safety specialist, waste industry experience an advantage but not essential * Relevant third level qualification * Strong system skills including Excel and other business applications * Ability to work to deadlines and prioritisation of key tasks * Ability to work across business units and report to owner managers. * Good communicator, comfortable liaising with and training employees and customers For further information please contact Orla Brennan 086 1720090 / orla.brennan@prismexecutive.ie

title: Executive Assistant - fixed term contract
description: **Line of Service** Internal Firm Services **Industry/Sector** Not Applicable **Specialism** IFS \- Internal Firm Services \- Other **Management Level** Associate **Job Description \& Summary** PwC Ireland\- Bring Your Talent, Grow Your Skills, Unlock Your Potential PwC offers an exciting opportunity to be part of a fast\-growing team of Executive Assistants (EAs) within the Core Administration Hub. Supported in part by the Business Administration Hubs, the EA will provide strategic and professional senior administrative support to designated Firm Partner(s). PwC’s Business Enablement Services (BES) Line of Service combines our support teams from across the firm who play pivotal roles in enabling our business delivery. Within BES, the Business Administration (BA) function delivers innovative workplace and business\-focused solutions across the firm through our collaborative team of experts. Our team ensures the effective operation and support of client service teams is in safe hands. We are passionate about customer service and deliver a wide range of services, from core administrative tasks to value\-add work for our stakeholders. This is a fixed term contract. BA is composed of four Hubs – Core Administration, Commercial Operations, Events and Travel, and Documentation. **This is a fantastic opportunity for the right individual to;** * Be an integral part of the Business Administration team providing dedicated support to Partners * Proactively develop their personal and technical skills to enhance their career * Expand their networks across PwC * Significantly broaden their understanding of the firm To be successful you will have the ability to liaise, influence and challenge effectively to build strong networks and relationships at all levels, both internally and externally. You will report into a Team Leader and will have a Career Coach to support your career aspirations and personal development. You will drive your own self development through gaining continuous feedback, evidencing an active commitment to upskilling in the role and taking ownership to plan and attend regular Career Coach Catch Ups. The role requires a high\-level of self\-management and personal responsibility coupled with the ability to prioritise, organise and manage tasks and workload. You will have a customer service mindset, good technical skills and the ability to keep up to date with current and emerging technology. **Purpose\-led work you’ll be part of** As an Associate, you’ll work as part of a team of problem solvers, helping to solve complex business issues from strategy to execution. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: **Responsible for:** * Management of an executive\-level BA service for Partners * Ownership of all enquiries and problems, resolving or escalating issues as appropriate. * Work with your stakeholders to ensure compliance with the Firm’s policies and procedures and meet the Firm deadlines. * Draft complex and sensitive communications on behalf of partners. * Proactively coordinate meeting logistics and anticipate conflicts. * Attend meetings as appropriate, providing detailed and actionable minutes. * Manage follow\-up on key action items and ensure accountability. * Project coordination \- attend project meetings (as requested) to understand requirements, delivery timescales, supporting actions and regular interaction with the project team. * Manage partner inboxes, prioritising critical communications. Handle sensitive documents with a high level of confidentiality and integrity. * Build and maintain strong client relationships, addressing issues proactively. * Liaise with the BA Hubs and Regional Offices as necessary. * Delegate transactional work where appropriate to effectively manage workloads. * Ability to draft stakeholder emails and engage with stakeholders to make decisions. * Support stakeholders and others in upskilling of Firmwide systems. **Additional responsibilities where required:** * Informal coaching junior team members * Involvement in team activities that contribute to personal \& Firm development * Other ad hoc responsibilities as required **Essential skills \& experience** * Secondary level education and a relevant Post second level qualification. * A minimum of 4 years of proven experience in a similar role, providing comprehensive administrative support to senior executives or management teams. * Proficiency in Microsoft 365 applications (Word, Outlook, PowerPoint, Excel). * Demonstrated strong project management and coordination skills, with the ability to effectively prioritise and manage multiple stakeholders' conflicting priorities. * Experience in handling sensitive information. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced, dynamic environment, demonstrating resilience and adaptability. * Demonstrated champion of Evolved PwC Professional behaviours. **Preferred Experience** * Excellent organisational skills and self\-motivation, demonstrating a commitment to continuous self\-development. * Strong influencing and communication skills, both verbal and written. * Ability to develop internal and external networks to support business objectives. * Flexibility and agility to adapt to changes and contribute effectively to system improvements or firm initiatives. * Role model for inclusivity, with an understanding of cultural differences and a commitment to diversity. **Unlock your potential with PwC Ireland** We believe that challenges are better solved together! We’re inspiring and empowering our people to change the world. Powered by the latest technology, you’ll be a part of amazing teams encouraged to collaborate and innovate in a way that creates powerful solutions and makes a positive impact. This purpose\-led work, and our continuous development and encouragement, will help unlock your potential and take your career to the next level. **Enjoy PwC’s perks** We reward your impact, and support your wellbeing, through a competitive compensation package, inclusive employee benefits and flexibility programs that will help you thrive in work and life. Learn more about us at Life@PwC. (https://www.pwc.ie/careers\-ie/life\-at\-pwc.html). **Being appreciated for being you** Our most valuable asset is our people and we grow stronger as we learn from one another. We are an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We believe this so much that we have signed up for the Business in the Community Elevate Pledge . ( https://www.bitc.ie/the\-leaders\-group\-on\-sustainability/inclusive\-workplace\-pledge/ ) You can learn more about our culture of belonging and explore our range of inclusive programmes, initiatives, employee resource groups and more at www.pwc.ie . Avoid the confidence gap; you do not have to match all the listed requirements exactly to apply, we’d love to hear from you! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please email us for more information. **Required Skills** **Optional Skills** Accepting Feedback, Accepting Feedback, Active Listening, Administrative Support, Agile Database Administration, Business Process Improvement, Calendar Management, Clerical Support, Collaborative Forecasting, Communication, Communications Management, Concur Travel, Conducting Research, Confidential Information Handling, Corporate Records Management, Correspondence Management, Credentialing Database, Customer Relationship Management, Customer Service Excellence, Customer Service Management, Data Entry, Deployment Coordination, Electronic Billing, Electronic Filing, Electronic Records Management {\+ 31 more} **Desired Languages** *(If blank, desired languages not specified)* **Travel Requirements** Not Specified **Available for Work Visa Sponsorship?** No **Government Clearance Required?** No **Job Posting End Date**

title: Facilities Specialist - Tech Environment
description: Facilities Specialist \- Tech Environment Dublin \- 5 days onsite This role is all about keeping the workplace running smoothly day to day. You'll help create a safe, efficient, and well\-maintained environment where people can do their best work. It's a great fit for someone who's into technology and the gaming industry, enjoys solving problems, and likes being hands\-on in a fast\-paced setting. You'll need to be adaptable, proactive, and comfortable figuring things out as you go. What you'll be doing \-Act as a go\-to person for workplace issues, making sure requests are handled quickly and efficiently \-Keep the office in great shape, from general upkeep to making sure systems and equipment are working properly \-Help with office moves, desk setups, and keeping floor plans and records up to date \-Keep an eye on building systems (like HVAC and BMS), troubleshoot issues, and escalate when needed \-Work closely with building management and technical teams to keep everything running smoothly \-Use internal systems to track requests, assets, and general facilities activity \-Manage the helpdesk, making sure all tickets are logged, followed up, and closed on time \-Support health \& safety standards and make sure processes are being followed \-Assist with admin tasks like raising POs and processing invoices \-Coordinate contractors and vendors, including scheduling, access, and overseeing work when required \-Keep track of equipment, assets, and warranties \-Work with different teams to solve problems and keep things moving \-Look for ways to improve how things are done, especially using tech and systems \-Help manage day\-to\-day office needs like supplies and on\-site services \-Get involved in projects and any other tasks that come up What we're looking for \-Some experience in Facilities, Workplace, or Operations \-A genuine interest in technology and modern workplaces \-An interest in or passion for the gaming industry is a big plus \-Familiarity with facilities or helpdesk systems (Maximo, Concept, or similar) is helpful \-Basic understanding of building systems (HVAC, BMS, etc.) is an advantage \-Someone who's practical, proactive, and enjoys solving problems \-Strong communication skills and a customer\-first mindset \-Ability to juggle multiple tasks and stay organised in a busy environment \-Comfortable using spreadsheets and working with data \-Awareness of Health \& Safety practices is a plus \-A team player who's flexible and ready to adapt as things change Adecco Ireland is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.

title: Field Engineer
description: Overview: Ergo are seeking IT Field Engineers for long\-term contracts in various locations nationwide. These are hands\-on, field\-based roles delivering onsite technical support across a diverse estate including end\-user devices, communications systems, networking, and infrastructure. You will work as part of a multi\-disciplinary engineering team, taking ownership of technical issues from diagnosis through to resolution while engaging directly with stakeholders to ensure effective service delivery. Requirements: \- 4 years experience in Field support \- Full Clean Driver’s license \- Own transport \- End User Computing: Desktop/laptop installation, imaging, domain configuration, and IP setup \- Mobile Devices: Setup and support of iOS and Android devices \- IP telephony (Unify, Avaya, Polycom), video conferencing, and collaboration tools \- Network patching and troubleshooting \- EU passport or Stamp 4 Visa \- Fluent in the English language Experience in the following an advantage: \- RAN / radio systems \- Antenna systems and CCTV Responsibilities: \- Deliver onsite installation, configuration, maintenance, and repair of IT and communications equipment \- Provide L2/L3 support, including advanced troubleshooting and root cause analysis \- Diagnose and resolve incidents within agreed service levels \- Conduct site surveys, testing, and quality checks \- Support network connectivity and infrastructure issues (LAN/WAN) \- Perform device builds, imaging, and deployments \- Support telephony, unified communications, and video conferencing systems \- Maintain accurate technical documentation and reporting, including development of knowledge base materials \- Liaise with end users, stakeholders, and technical teams to ensure effective communication \- Operate in line with all health, safety, and operational standards Apply today for more information.

title: Field Service Engineer
location: Lucan and Pettycanon, South Dublin, Ireland
description: **Step into a career with ASM, where cutting edge technology meets collaborative culture.** For over 55 years ASM has been ahead of what’s next, at the forefront of innovation and what’s technologically possible. With more than 4,500 ASMers representing 70 nationalities, our people and our advanced semiconductor devices are playing a crucial role in trends such as 5G, cloud computing, AI, and autonomous driving. But we’re more than just a tech company. We value diversity, inclusion and sustainability as we strive to make a positive impact on the world. Our development programs help support your growth, shaping your future and pushing the boundaries of innovation to unleash potential. ASM is a leading global supplier of products, services, and materials for semiconductor processing. For more than half a century, innovation has been at the core of everything we do. Our smart, ambitious people are dedicated to creating cutting\-edge solutions for the world’s leading semiconductor providers. Every day we push the development of next\-generation computer\-chip technology, always staying a few steps ahead of what’s next. The talented, enthusiastic people at ASM are just like you: dedicated to improving people’s lives and unlocking new potential. With our collaborative approach to R\&D, we advance key semiconductor technologies and platforms like ALD, epitaxy, PEALD, PECVD, and vertical furnaces. And we have recently entered the promising high\-growth market: silicon carbide epitaxy. Year after year, our innovations help to make chips smaller, faster, and more powerful. In fact, you will find our technology in every aspect of modern life: in AI, medical equipment, 5G, smartphones, autonomous driving, and more. For installations and commissioning of our systems as well as the associated maintenance and repairs, we are looking for a **Field Service Engineer.** **Primary Functions** * Installations, qualification, preventative maintenance and remedial repair maintenance on advanced electro\-mechanical semiconductor capital equipment at one of ASM’s key customer locations in Ireland, US, Asia or Middle East * Project management of key projects at customer sites to install ASM’s latest NPI equipment. * Perform tool\-down repair support in complex situations; work in variedly formed teams; develop action plans and presentations for problem resolutions. * As a field service engineer you work onsite at various customer locations in a cleanroom environment. * Create and maintain a high level of customer satisfaction through advanced customer service skills, implementing permanent solutions while maintaining a "safety first" mentality. * Comprehensive and swift reporting Travel within the ASM Europe region we have many customers scattered throughout the region, and therefore the FSE role is a high travel position, up to 75% of annual time (divided between 50% working locally at customer site and 50% travelling to other locations). Your customer base will be in Ireland and surrounding areas (predominantly Europe). Due to ASM’s growing international presence and customer base, the occasional business trip to North America or Asia might be necessary. **We could be a great match for each other if you have the following:** * Bachelor's degree in mechanical, Electrical, Electronic or Mechatronic Engineering or relevant high school technical diploma * Ideally, already a first experience in field service/customer support and has acquired knowledge in installation and startup of systems, preferably in the semiconductor industry * Good hands\-on skills * Excellent communication skills in English. Any other European languages are a bonus * Working software knowledge of MS office, SAP based software * You have a valid driver’s license **Our Offer** * Your chance to work for one of the leading suppliers of semiconductor process equipment globally * Varied and dynamic international business environment * A supportive, collaborative team\-based environment * A competitive salary package including a base salary, bonus, Pension and private health care. * Career development support * Advanced equipment training will be provide at our global training center **PELASE NOTE: we can only consider candidates for this role who have the right to work in Ireland.** At ASM we are committed to supporting our people, enabling them to develop their talents and perform at their best. We actively promote an inclusive and diverse culture that fosters trust and transparency. Our people are our power, and we value the differences between everyone because it is what make us better as a team. By embracing a wide range of perspectives, we enable a culture of continuous exploration and adoption of new ideas. At every step, we know that it is our valued people who enable us to deliver excellent products and service to our clients – and to make a meaningful difference in society. So join us on our journey and unleash the power of an open mind. Discover how you can be part of our exciting future and apply today! To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram and YouTube. **Apply Today To Be Part Of What’s Next.** We make the tech that enables the chips in devices which improve lives around the world. We do this with an eye to the future, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible through cutting\-edge innovation, and driving the next wave of technological breakthroughs that shape how we live, work, and connect. To learn more about ASM, find us at asm.com and on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, X and YouTube. *ASM is an equal opportunity employer and considers qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, nationality, social or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender, gender identify or expression, marital status, pregnancy, political affiliation, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.*

title: Finance Intern
description: **Description** At West, we’re a dedicated team that is connected by a purpose to improve patient lives that has been at the center of our Company for more than a century. Our story began when Herman O. West solved the problem of supplying penicillin in mass quantities to the US Government during World War 2\. Through our work to deliver thousands of life\-saving and life\-enhancing injectable medicines to millions of patients daily, West’s indelible mark on the healthcare industry has just begun. A name started our story. How will yours help write our future? There’s no better place to join an inclusive community of professionals with opportunities for lifelong learning, growth and development. Supported by benefit programs, we empower the physical, mental, emotional and financial health of our team members and their families. We believe in giving back to help those in need in the communities where we live and awork. And are equally committed to creating a healthier environment and planet through our sustainability efforts. **Job Summary** To position itself to meet the requirements of continuing rapid business growth and future market development, West Pharmaceutical Services have developed a transformation strategy of standardization, simplification and centralization of global accounting operations. In accordance with that strategy, we are establishing a Global Financial Centre of Excellence in Dublin, Ireland. Working closely with the Finance Manager/Lead, in this role, the successful candidate will be part of a shared service team driving automation opportunities across the finance function. As a Finance Intern, you will support the team in various financial tasks and projects. You will gain practical experience in accounting controllership function. This internship is an excellent opportunity to develop your skills and gain insights into the finance operations of a dynamic company and exploitation of opportunities offered by new information technologies. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** * US GAAP Month\-End Close: Support the controllership team in executing the monthly close process, including journal entries, account reconciliations, variance analysis, and preparation of supporting schedules to ensure accurate and timely financial reporting. * Statutory Reporting Platform Implementation (Europe Local GAAP/IFRS): Assist with data mapping, template configuration, system testing, and coordination with European finance teams to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of local statutory reporting. * Financial Reporting \& Analysis: Contribute to the preparation of financial statements, management reports, and analytical reviews to support transparency and data\-driven business decisions. * Audit \& Compliance Support: Provide documentation and analytical assistance for internal and external audits, ensuring compliance with US GAAP, Local GAAP/IFRS, and internal control requirements. * Process Improvement \& Documentation: Document accounting processes and identify opportunities to enhance efficiency, standardization, automation, and reporting effectiveness within the controllership function. * Ledger \& Financial Operations: Support general ledger maintenance and reconciliations using digital tools and AI\-enabled insights to improve accuracy and control. * Advanced Financial Analysis: Perform in\-depth financial analysis using data analytics techniques to generate actionable insights and develop innovative reporting solutions. * Process Automation \& Digital Enablement: Identify and implement automation opportunities leveraging AI and digital technologies to streamline financial operations. * Project Support: Participate in finance\-related projects assigned by management, applying digital strategies and analytical insights. * Additional Responsibilities: Perform other duties as assigned by the Team Leader or Manager. ****Education**** * Completed a minimum of two years of a Bachelor’s degree program in Accounting, Finance, or a closely related discipline. **Preferred Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Proficient in MS Office applications * Team player, positive and flexible attitude * Excellent communication skills i.e. ability to communicate and work effectively with a wide range of stakeholders * Power BI knowledge * The ability to work on their own initiative as part of a hybrid model. * Able to comply with the company’s safety policy at all times. * Able to always comply with the company’s quality policy. **Pay Transparency** The hourly rate for this position is €14\.15\. Total target compensation is determined using objective and gender\-neutral criteria, including relevant experience, qualifications, skills, market benchmarks, geographic location, and the scope and responsibilities of the role. This position may be eligible for variable compensation and other incentive programs in accordance with the terms of the applicable plans within the country of employment. In addition, team members are eligible to participate in the company’s benefits and total rewards programs applicable to their location and employment status. West is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. If you have a special need that requires accommodation in order to apply to West, please send an email to Apply.Accommodation@westpharma.com . Where permitted by law, an offer of employment with West Pharmaceutical Services, or any of its subsidiary or affiliate companies, is contingent upon the satisfactory completion of background screening.

title: Financial Advisor
description: Our client is a well\-established financial planning and wealth management business seeking an ambitious Financial Planner to join their growing team. This is a client\-facing role suited to an advisor who enjoys combining technical financial planning with relationship management and business development. The position offers the opportunity to work with an established client portfolio while also developing new client relationships. The business places strong emphasis on delivering a streamlined client experience, supported by modern technology and efficient onboarding processes, allowing advisers to focus on high\-quality financial advice and long\-term planning outcomes. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide independent financial advice across a broad client base. * Develop tailored financial plans aligned to client goals. * Use cashflow modelling tools to illustrate scenarios and recommendations. * Advise across: * Pensions and Retirement Planning * Investments and Wealth Management * Savings Strategies * Protection Solutions **Client Management \& Development** * Manage an existing portfolio of clients with ongoing reviews. * Build long\-term, trusted client relationships. * Identify and pursue new business opportunities. * Maintain high levels of client service and engagement. **Collaboration \& Professional Standards** * Work closely with internal teams to ensure smooth onboarding and service delivery. * Stay up to date with regulatory changes, market trends, and product developments. * Maintain strong professional and compliance standards at all times. **About You** You are a motivated and client\-focused Financial Planner with strong technical capability and a proactive approach to client service and business development. You are confident in explaining complex financial concepts clearly and enjoy building long\-term client relationships based on trust and performance. You are organised, commercially aware, and comfortable balancing both advisory and growth responsibilities. **Essential Requirements** * QFA qualified. * Minimum 2 years’ experience in financial planning or financial advisory role. * Strong knowledge of: * Pensions * Investments * Protection products * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Proven ability to meet or exceed performance targets. * Ability to manage a busy client portfolio effectively. **Desirable Requirements** * Experience within financial planning, wealth management, or brokerage environments. * Exposure to cashflow modelling tools (e.g. Voyant or similar). * Experience working with a broad range of client profiles. **What’s on Offer** * Competitive salary and attractive bonus structure. * Flexible working options, including hybrid arrangements. * Pension and protection benefits. * Generous annual leave entitlement. * Strong emphasis on professional development and further education support. * Opportunity to grow within a high\-performing advisory environment.

title: Financial Advisor
description: We are currently working with a Wealth Management firm who are looking to hire a Financial Advisor to join their team in Dublin. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. Responsibilities of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive client focused service. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes and market developments Requirements of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Fully QFA qualified * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets and industry practices * Demonstrable experience in financial advisory, with a proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * A high level of commercial awareness For more information on this **Financial Advisor** Job or to discuss in more detail, contact Aoife Stokes at astokes@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly below.

title: Financial Services Scholarship Programme
description: **Job Description** **Financial Services Scholarship Programme** **Location: Dublin** **Program Starting in September 2026** **Closing Date: 3rd July 2026** **Are you ready to launch your career in a global financial services organisation?** Fidelity Investments has an exciting opportunity for students finishing their Leaving Certificate or FETAC/QQI Level 5\. Our Financial Services Scholarship Programme, in partnership with Griffith College, will give you the skills needed to launch a career in global financial services. **The Team** Our operations group provides middle and back\-office services to Fidelity businesses through innovation, dedication, and commitment to excellence. You will be placed in one of our teams who support the processing of financial transactions through the trade lifecycle including: Account Services, Settlements, Asset Services, Tax Services, Corporate Actions, Security Reference, Quality \& Performance, Index and Portfolio. There are many diverse teams comprised of highly enthusiastic and driven individuals who trust in the power of innovation and teamwork to achieve great things. Our focus is on making our financial expertise broadly accessible and effective in helping people live the lives they want. **The Programme** Upon joining in September 2026, successful candidates will have a full\-time position and join a team within Fidelity’s Operations group in Dublin, while undertaking a fully funded part\-time degree in Business with Griffith College. Your experience will begin with onboarding to Fidelity and learning the skills needed to be successful in your role. Fidelity will provide full support in all aspects of your training, onboarding, and role upskilling. Over the next 3 years, you will complete a business degree part\-time with a blend of learning in college and self\-directed. ***Work Full Time – Earn*** * 40 \- hours per week ***Study Part Time \- Learn*** * BA (Hons) in Business * Tuition fees will be covered as part of this scholarship programme **Entry Requirements** * Currently pursuing or recently completed Leaving Certificate or FETAC/QQI Level 5\. * You are required to have the following Leaving Certificate results upon completion: 2 x H5 and 4 x O6, including a minimum O6 in Math’s and a language (which can be English, Irish or 3rd Language) * FETAC/QQI Level 5 for level 8 \- minimum full level 5 award to include a minimum of 3 distinctions and a pass in all other remaining subjects. **The Value You Bring** Are you the kind of person who: * Enjoys working with others and in a team environment * Works well within a team and knows how to respond to people * Is highly motivated and punctual * Likes to solve problems * Is detail orientated * Is customer focused or has experience working with customers * Has experience completing projects where you have worked within a group * Is enthusiastic with a strong interest in developing a career in financial services * Has created presentations and delivered them to an audience **What’s in it for you?** A career with Fidelity Ireland is filled with potential and you’re empowered to build the kind of future you want as you help us redefine operational excellence. There’s lots on offer. From making a difference through community volunteerism, to further professional accreditation, or fuelling your passion by joining one of our many employee advocacy groups. We have an amazing package of benefits and programs designed to help you thrive. As part of the scholarship programme, you can expect the following: * A degree in business * Comprehensive benefits package including + Healthcare Plan and Employee Assistance Program + Lifestyle benefit – an annual €500 tax\-free pre\-paid credit card + Subsidized onsite canteen + Access to lifestyle savings and discounts + Performance related bonus + Pension with company contribution based on age and tenure + Dedicated induction and onboarding + Assigned mentor and buddy + Friendly, professional work environment **Category** Operations

title: Financial Services Tax Partner - FATCA & CRS & AEOI
description: Director / Senior Manager – Global Information Reporting (FATCA / CRS / AEOI) Big 4 Firm \| Hybrid \| Nationwide (Ireland) International tax transparency keeps evolving — and clients need advisers who can turn complex rules into practical, defensible reporting. Our client's Global Information Reporting / AEOI team supports leading financial services and corporate clients with FATCA, CRS and broader AEOI compliance, governance, and strategy. If you’re comfortable leading high\-profile engagements, shaping governance frameworks, and being the person clients ring first when deadlines or regulations shift, this is built for you. What you’ll be doing * Advising clients on FATCA/CRS obligations and compliance strategy * Leading end\-to\-end FATCA/CRS reporting and due diligence projects (including data interpretation and validation) * Designing and reviewing compliance frameworks, readiness assessments, and governance / risk controls * Supporting audit queries and regulatory interactions with clear, technical responses * Producing technical updates, client communications, and contributing to business development * Coaching junior colleagues and driving internal knowledge\-sharing What success looks like in your first 6–12 months * Become a trusted primary contact across a portfolio for FATCA/CRS matters * Deliver timely, accurate filings and well\-managed client projects * Strengthen client programmes through practical governance and control enhancements * Track regulatory developments and contribute to training and thought leadership What you’ll bring * Professional qualification: ACA / CTA (AITI) / equivalent, plus a relevant degree (Accounting/Finance/Law or similar) * Strong experience in tax advisory or financial services, with solid exposure to FATCA/CRS/AEOI * Strong technical grasp of Irish Revenue and OECD reporting requirements and how to apply them in practice * Proven ability to lead projects, manage stakeholders, and build long\-term client relationships * Tax technology / automation experience is a plus Location \& working model This role can be based in any Irish office, with a hybrid working model balancing client needs, office collaboration and flexibility. What you’ll get A competitive package plus strong investment in development: learning \& training, coaching, wellbeing supports, pension and enhanced leave — in an inclusive culture that expects high standards and backs you to grow.

title: Flight Dispatcher
description: ASL Aviation Holdings is a global aviation services holding company with a fleet of more than 160 aircraft, focused on cargo and passenger airline operations, aircraft leasing and maintenance. Headquartered in Dublin Ireland, the Group includes five wholly owned airlines; ASL Airlines Ireland, ASL Airlines Belgium, ASL Airlines France, ASL Airlines United Kingdom and most recently, ASL Airlines Australia. The Group also includes associate and joint venture airlines FlySafair in South Africa, K\-Mile Asia in Thailand \& QuikJet in India. ASL’s airlines operate cargo networks for the world’s leading express parcel integrators and online retailers. Our group, joint\-venture and associate airlines also operate scheduled and charter cargo and passenger services under ASL’s own airline brands and on behalf of airline ACMI customers and postal services. Globally, ASL has a team of 3,000 people of 51 different nationalities. The Group’s vision is to be the aviation service provider of choice through excellence, experience, and expertise. **Position:** ------------- The purpose of the role is to analyse, control and report activities related to the Operational Flight Plan and Air Traffic Service Flight Plan in accordance with the rules implemented by aviation regulatory authorities and company procedures. Ensure the most reliable, safe and efficient airline operations. **Key Accountabilities:** * Prepare Operational Flight Plans for all sectors and ensure compliant routes are filed, whilst monitoring current Weather and Notam information that may affect the operation. * Work closely with the LMC so that OFPs can be adjusted to compensate for any aircraft defects that may have a penalty on the operation. * Produce all documentation required by flight crew including Met Reports and Forecasts, Notams and OFP’s. * Promote and implement the company SMS to ensure that the prevention of accidents is a priority. * Keep a flight watch on the Company aircraft and any other flights for which the company has a responsibility. * Keep the OCC Manager and Crew appraised of any foreseeable disruption to the programme whilst using the most efficient and economically viable option. * Ensure all adhoc aircraft charters and positioning flights have been allocated with the required OFP resources. Prepare a recap of the Nights Operation. * Maintain competence on the basis of continued education and training in their area of responsibility. The company will facilitate this process. **Requirements:** ----------------- **Qualifications:** * Knowledge of Meteorology, Navigation, Aircraft Mass and Balance, Air Traffic management, Aircraft performance, Air Law Regulations. * Knowledge of CAE Flight Plan Manager or similar systems desirable * Recognised FAA or EASA\-Ops qualification an advantage **Experience:** * 5 years Airline experience in European and ETOPS flight planning is desirable. * Knowledge of CHMI and CFMU functions. * Experience in an OCC environment. Candidates must be eligible to live and work in the EU. **We offer:** a competitive salary, 24 shifts annual leave (increasing with continuous service) and a range of other benefits, including car parking, generous employer pension contribution and life assurance cover. As part of our commitment to our IBEC KeepWell accreditation we offer: employee assistance program, subsidized gym membership, cycle to work scheme, tax saver scheme, and access to The ASL Way Learning Academy. Don't miss out on this exciting opportunity to join ASL Airlines Ireland and take your career to new heights! **Company:** ASL Airlines Ireland **Reports to:** OCC Supervisor **Department:** Operations, ASL Head office, Swords, Co. Dublin, Ireland. **Contract:** Permanent **Shift Pattern:** 4 shifts on, 4 shifts off (includes day and night shifts) **Shift Duration:** 12 hours **Other information:** ---------------------- **Dear Candidate,** **Thank you for your interest in ASL.** **Once you submit your CV and cover letter for us, the system automatically sends you an 'Application Confirmation' and a ‘Registration Confirmation’ email which contains a link to activate your account and to choose your password. We also send you the interview invitations via in\-recruiting.** **We kindly ask you to please check your spam folder as the emails might be ending up there. You may look for the following title(s) and E\-mail address.** **Title(s): Registration Confirmation / Application Confirmation** **E\-mail address: recruitment@aslaviationgroup.com** **Kind Regards,** **ASL HR Team**

title: Forward Deployed Engineer (Perm, Ireland, Remote + Travel)
description: This is a **full\-time, permanent opportunity** for candidates **based in Ireland.** This position also requires **frequent travel to client sites within Europe** , with **travel expenses covered by the company** . **About Nearform** Nearform is an independent team of data \& AI experts, engineers, and designers who build intelligent digital solutions and capability at pace. We create AI\-enabled solutions that enhance digital experiences, empower developers, and deliver measurable results. In these ways, we partner with ambitious enterprises to deliver enduring impact. Our deep expertise in solving the world's most complex digital problems, along with our collaborative, people\-first approach, enables enterprises to build breakthrough products and modernise legacy systems by unleashing the power of AI. Today, our team of 500 experts in 20\+ countries is trusted by leading enterprises including Lululemon, Puma, Sun Life, Starbucks, Travelex, Virgin Media 02, and Walmart. **About the role** As a Forward Deployed Engineer, you will help build, implement, and deploy agentic platforms within client environments. Your role will focus on building and deploying scalable AI\-driven solutions, enabling organisations to operationalise LLM\-powered agents across products, processes, and teams. You will work closely with client stakeholders, technical leaders, and delivery teams to build, and roll out agentic capabilities that integrate seamlessly with existing enterprise systems and frameworks. **What we are looking for in this person:** * Significant experience delivering at a Senior Engineer level. Our Seniors typically have at least 8 years of commercial experience. * Experience designing and building agentic platforms using Python and modern AI frameworks. * Architect LLM orchestration using frameworks such as LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, and related technologies. * Implement multi\-agent systems supporting tool use, workflow orchestration, memory, and decision\-making. * Design and deploy Retrieval\-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines integrating enterprise data sources. * Integrate agentic platforms with client systems, including APIs, data platforms, cloud services, and enterprise applications. * The ability to configure Kubernetes and deploy workloads. * Design deployment architectures across cloud, on\-premise, and hybrid environments. * Ability to collaborate efficiently in teams of technical and non\-technical individuals. * Comfort working in a dynamic environment with evolving objectives and direct iteration with users. * Strong consulting experience to plan, execute, and deliver complex client engagements, solving real business problems. **Nice to have:** * Hands\-on experience working with Golang * Hands\-on experience in the Frontend working with React * Deep understanding of Kubernetes **Benefits \& Perks:** * **Annual Company Bonus \-** We all help Nearform to hit company goals so we all receive a share of the profits on an annual basis in line with company performance. * **Work Remotely and Flexibly \-** We have a genuine dedication to work/life balance. Our flexible working culture allows you to work around what matters \- school run, no problem! * **Paid Time Off Package \-** We offer an annual leave of 24 days plus public holidays. We also offer sick leave, marriage leave and many more. * **Remote Working Allowance \-** Every 2 years, you will have a budget of up to €1250 (equivalent in GBP) to help you set up a comfortable and productive workspace in addition to your essential equipment provided by Nearform when you join. * **Training and Development Allowance \-** We understand the importance of continuously learning so we offer an allowance of up to €1000 (equivalent in GBP) you can use to upskill yourself. * **Healthcare \-** It's important to always take care of your health, so we offer additional private healthcare here at Nearform. If you wish to learn more about the plan offered feel free to reach out to our team. * **Pension \& Insurances** \- We offer a pension match of up to 5% and Income Protection and Death in Service for peace of mind. **Our Interview Process** * + **Talent Call** (30 mins via Zoom) An introductory conversation with a member of our Talent Team to learn more about your background, experience, and career goals. We'll also provide a high\-level overview of Nearform and answer any questions you may have. This step helps us assess initial alignment. + **Technical Assessment** (1 hour via Zoom) A live coding challenge with one of our trained interviewers. You'll be asked to build a working solution to a problem via screen sharing, demonstrating your Python expertise, problem\-solving ability, and coding best practices in a real\-time environment. + **Hiring Manager Interview** (45 mins via Zoom) A conversation with a Hiring Manager focused on your approach to teamwork, client collaboration, and problem\-solving in an enterprise development setting. We'll also share more details about Nearform and the potential projects you may join. *There may also be an additional interview with the client depending on the project* **Ready to Join Us?** If you're passionate about building high\-quality applications, solving complex challenges, and driving innovation in enterprise development, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now via our careers page at Nearform and be part of a team that values technical excellence, collaboration, and continuous learning. *Nearform is committed to shaping a better world in all that we do. Our global team is built based on respect, inclusivity, diversity and excellence.* *If you require any adjustments to the interview process as part of your application, please let someone from our Talent Team know so they can help.* Salary range \- €75\-€100,000 \+ bonus \& benefits

title: Freelance Business Consultant
description: Role: Freelance Business Consultant (Commission\-Based) Location: 100% Remote (Open to candidates in US, UK, Europe, and Arab/GCC countries) Company: Essemvee Technology Services Limited Industry: IT Consulting, Cloud Engineering \& DevOps Automation About Essemvee Essemvee Technology Services Limited is a high\-growth technology consultancy specializing in end\-to\-end cloud infrastructure engineering, DevOps automation, AI/MLOps pipelines, and enterprise software architecture. We deliver tailored, high\-performance solutions that eliminate operational friction, optimize cloud infrastructure, and drive digital transformation for scaling businesses. To fuel our international market expansion, we are inviting motivated freelancers, independent consultants, and driven business development professionals across the US, UK, Europe, and the Middle East to join us as Independent Business Development Consultants. Work completely remotely, manage your own time, and earn highly competitive, uncapped commission\-based income by helping us expand our global enterprise client base. Role Details Position: Freelance Business Development Consultant Work Mode: 100% Remote (Flexible hours adjusted to your local market) Compensation: 5%–15% Commission Per Closed Deal (Uncapped earning potential) Territory Focus: Target enterprise accounts within your respective region (US, UK, EU, or Arab countries/GCC). What You Will Do Regional Market Prospecting: Identify and approach enterprise clients within your region (FinTech, SaaS, E\-Commerce, Tech Startups, or large\-scale legacy enterprises) requiring cloud modernization or DevOps support. Vendor Registration: Navigate and register Essemvee as a trusted technical vendor with target corporate organizations and procurement systems. Technical Pitching: Present Essemvee’s core advisory services—including Cloud Infrastructure Migration, CI/CD Pipeline Automation, MLOps, and Site Reliability Engineering (SRE)—to engineering directors, CTOs, and CIOs. Proposal Collaboration: Work closely with our core technical consulting team to frame high\-impact technical proposals and respond to enterprise RFPs. Deal Closure: Drive regional leads from initial outreach through negotiation to signed business contracts. Compensation \& Structure Commission\-Based Only (No fixed base salary). Earn a premium 5% to 15% commission based on the total closed contract value. Commissions are paid out globally via international transfer immediately following successful client milestone payments. Complete Autonomy: You control your hours, your target pipeline, and your outreach strategies. Who Can Apply Legal Status: Must be legally allowed to operate as an independent contractor or freelancer within your country of residence (e.g., W9/1099 in the US, Sole Trader/Ltd in the UK/EU, Freelance/Consultant status in GCC countries). Communication: Exceptional professional English language skills (written and spoken) are mandatory. Knowledge of regional languages (such as Arabic, German, or French) to unlock local corporate markets is a significant advantage. Background: Prior experience or a deep foundational interest in IT Consulting, Cloud\-Native Ecosystems, Cybersecurity, or B2B Enterprise Sales is highly preferred. Tools: Must have your own remote workspace setup, laptop, and reliable internet connectivity. How to Apply Please submit your application or LinkedIn profile link via LinkedIn Easy Apply. In your cover message, briefly mention your city/country of residence and your specific target industry networks.

title: Full Price - IRL Sales Team Member
description: Why join us? We’re more than just a shoe brand—we have been at the forefront of innovative shoemaking since its foundation in 1825\. That’s right! 200 years! If you have a great attitude, love working with people, we want to hear from you! **About the role:** We are looking for a reliable and enthusiastic Sales Associate to provide a great consumer experience in store whilst delivering set targets and KPIs. Effectively selling shoes and accessories through the delivery of the appropriate Clarks selling models. Support in the day\-to\-day maintenance of high retail standards both front and back of house. **We’re looking for someone who will:** * Consistently deliver a great brand experience, including utilising technology to enhance the consumers’ experience and keeping up to date with product knowledge. * Support the achievement of key store KPI’s by delivering sales at target levels set by store Management team. * Maintain the sales floor and back of house areas in order to deliver high brand and retail standards * Flexibility to work a 7\-day shift pattern, including weekends and bank holidays. * Follow cash and stock security procedures to Company standards. * Maintain visual merchandising standards and replenish as required. Benefits We reward our Sales Associates with a comprehensive package, including: * Retail Trust * Life Assurance * Clarks Discount Card * Holiday Pay * Eyecare Vouchers * Clarks Workplace Pension Scheme * Potential to win store bonus if overachieve set KPI’s Join us and bring your positive energy to Clarks – where every day is an adventure!

title: Full Time Retail Sales Consultant- Liffey Valley Shopping Centre
description: Role Title: Retail Sales Consultant – Full Time –Liffey Valley Business Area: 01 About This Role: As a **Retail Sales Consultant** at eir, you will be a face of our brand—engaging with customers to understand their needs and providing tailored broadband and mobile solutions that exceed expectations. In this dynamic retail role, every interaction is an opportunity to represent eir’s values, grow our customer base, and contribute to the success of your store. Enjoy earning a monthly salary along with additional earnings from our attractive commission structure. If you’re a people person with a passion for tech and a drive to hit targets, we’d love to hear from you. Why This Role: This is more than just a retail job—it’s your chance to build a career in an exciting and fast\-paced industry. As a **Retail Sales Consultant** , you’ll: * Deliver outstanding customer service. * Work in a target\-driven environment that rewards achievement. * Be part of a team where your input is valued and your success is celebrated. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a busy setting and enjoys sales and working with people. You'll receive full training, so even if you’re new to telecoms, you’ll be set up for success. **Benefits Include** * Earn your annual salary of €28,798 \+ Attractive commission structure! * Continuous support, learning and opportunities for advancement. * Enhanced earning potential through various staff incentives. * Staff offer. * Free eir Wellness Programme \& Employee Assistance Programme. * €500 Employee Referral Scheme. * Discounted healthcare plan. * Pension scheme contribution. * 22 days annual leave. * Generous family friendly policies, including fertility, pregnancy loss, menopause and others. * Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion. * Paid leave benefits incl. maternity and sick leave benefits. * Tax saver tickets and bike to work scheme. Expectations From The Role: As a **Retail Sales Consultant** , your key responsibilities will include: * Customer Engagement: Build rapport with customers, understand their needs, and recommend the most suitable mobile and broadband solutions. * Sales Delivery: Consistently meet or exceed individual sales targets while contributing to the store’s overall performance. * Customer Experience: Handle queries and issues efficiently, ensuring every customer leaves satisfied. * Store Operations: Support daily administrative tasks, including stock control, data entry, and cash handling. * Product \& Policy Knowledge: Stay up\-to\-date with eir’s product offerings, promotions, and store policies to ensure accurate and compliant service delivery. * Market Awareness: Keep informed of competitor offerings so you can provide customers with the most competitive and relevant information. Flexibility is essential—you’ll be required to work evenings and weekends in line with store trading hours. Requirements For A Successful Application: **Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Confidence in engaging with a variety of customers and understanding their needs. * Clear, effective communication skills with a strong ability to explain products and services. * A positive, target\-driven mindset with a passion for providing great service. * Interest in mobile phones, broadband, and emerging technologies. **Qualifications** * 1–2 years of experience in retail or sales is desirable, but relevant life experience will also be considered. * A strong customer focus and a proactive attitude are key to success in this role. Others: Our purpose is to **connect for a better Ireland.** Our ambition is **to be the number one choice for telecommunications and technology solutions, delivering for our customers today and into the future.** **Our new values and behaviours reflect both who we are and who we strive to become. They are the way that we bring our purpose to life in eir.** **We are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, and we will work with you to meet your needs.** If successful in the interview process, eir reserves the right to conduct appropriate suitability checks in relation to prospective employees including but not limited to reference checking and/or other searches using publicly available information.

title: Full Time Warehouse Operative - Damastown
description: **The Company** EZ Living Furniture was founded in Galway in 1998\. We are a family run Irish retailer operating nationwide, as well as a full online shopping experience offering delivery throughout Ireland. We are the largest independent Furniture Retailer in Ireland employing over 350 people. An exciting opportunity has arisen to join EZ Living Furniture's logistics operations team in our Damastown, Dublin Warehouse The successful candidate will play an essential role in our large fast paced warehouse operation. Warehouse experience is an advantage but not essential. We will provide training to the right person who can demonstrate motivation and a willingness to learn. If you are interested in taking on a new challenge at a growing company, we encourage you to apply today. **Responsibilities** * Carrying out the daily order picking and dispatching of goods. * Unloading containerized goods on a daily basis. * Bar coding and scanning all stock movements from goods in to dispatch. * Understand, implement, and carry out all Company policies, procedures, and Health \& Safety standards. **Skills And Qualifications** * Experience in a busy warehouse is an advantage but more important is that the candidate demonstrates initiative and an aptitude to learn quickly. * Current forklift license for reach and counter balance (if not, training will be provided). * Current manual handling certificate (if not, training will be provided). * Excellent attention to detail and accuracy when loading and unloading goods. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills. * Please note that this role will require a significant amount of manual handling. * Be comfortable working at heights **WHY JOIN US?** **Look us up on Glassdoor\- rated 4\.6/5 please read our reviews** **Benefits** * Up to 23 days holidays per year * Fun, fast paced \& high energy work environment * Culture of developing and promoting from within the company * Our Generous staff discounts * Our pension scheme * Our Bonus Scheme * Our Educational Support Scheme * Our Discounted corporate rate health care * Our free Employee Assistance Programme * Our wellbeing initiatives like our Bike to work scheme * Career Development Opportunities * Continuous professional development with dedicated training resources **\#Jobs**

title: Full-Stack AI Engineer - Automation Builder
description: I'm working with a fast\-moving specialty pharma group operating across 40\+ markets, with deep expertise in rare diseases including haematology\-oncology. They partner with manufacturers to commercialize life\-saving medicines quickly and efficiently, from regulatory approval through to patient access. Reporting to the Head of AI \& Digitalisation, you will be Employee \#1 in the AI Hub. Your first mission: build our on\-prem AI sandbox on an NVIDIA DGX Spark unit (128 GB RAM, 4 TB SSD), then ship 5\-7 production\-ready AI automations in 2026\. This is a greenfield role—no legacy systems, no bureaucracy, just you, the DGX box, LLM APIs, and a mandate to prototype fast and fail forward. You will build the core stack (n8n, PostgreSQL, vector DB, Langfuse, Prometheus/Grafana, Ollama) and create LLM\-powered agents for regulatory submissions, HCP targeting, pharmacovigilance, and internal ops—automations that will 3X our capacity without adding headcount. **Requirements** * Full\-stack development: Python (FastAPI/Flask) \+ JavaScript/TypeScript (React/Vue/Node.js) * API design \& integration: RESTful APIs, webhooks, OAuth2, API gateways, rate limiting * LLM integration: OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter APIs, prompt engineering, function calling, streaming * Database \& storage: PostgreSQL, vector DBs (Qdrant/Pinecone/Weaviate), Redis/caching patterns * Workflow automation: n8n, Zapier, Make.com, or similar low\-code/no\-code platforms * Linux/Docker: comfortable with SSH, docker\-compose, systemd, basic networking

title: Fullstack Engineer, Internal Tools
description: Who we are **About Stripe** Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career. **About The Team** The Collaboration team helps Stripe employees work smarter and efficiently, get to know each other, and request and receive help from one another. The team is nimble and creative. We work across a wide stack—on any given day, we discuss user interface design, data models, API design, infrastructure maintenance, or all of the above. We're excited to innovate in a space that traditionally isn't thought of as at the forefront of innovation. Being a small team, we carefully choose what we work on to have the highest leverage. This is not always easy: we work very cross\-functionally, at times partnering with various groups from engineering to Communications to top management of the company. But one of our greatest rewards is getting to sit next to and see the impact our work has on our users: our fellow Stripe employees. What you'll do As an engineer on the Collaboration team, you'll be a key member of a team that acts as a force multiplier on the internal tools that Stripe uses. You'll focus on building innovative GenAI\-powered experiences that help Stripe employees be more productive across various tasks and activities. You'll build these experiences by collaborating with multiple other teams across Stripe and experimenting with different ideas and the newest technologies. **Responsibilities** * Work with a team of smart engineers who put users first. * Own solutions from end to end, managing complexity and engaging directly with stakeholders to think through everything from success metrics, to the details of products and tools that you build. * Use both qualitative and quantitative metrics to determine priorities and measure effectiveness. * Uphold high standards for engineering and product quality and mentor newer team members to do the same. Who you are We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement. **Minimum Requirements** * You have 6\+ years of industry experience. * You have 2\+ years of industry experience building rich web experiences using modern frameworks such as React, Vue, etc. * You enjoy working across a full stack—from user experience, to workflow automations, to reliability. * You enjoy working directly with users and building creative solutions that address their problems. **Preferred Qualifications** * You have a passion for enabling people to do their best work. * You have an interest in partnering with creative, non\-technical teams, such as the teams responsible for creative communications and publishing at Stripe. * You have a track record of shipping LLM\-powered experiences with a strong understanding of how underlying technologies work. * You have industry experience working with Ruby, TypeScript, and React. * You never lose sight of the end\-user, and enjoy the quick feedback loops made possible by working (sometimes literally) next to your users. * You enjoy working together with your team toward common goals, but are able to drive your work forward autonomously. * You work very well cross\-functionally, and are able to think rigorously and make hard decisions and tradeoffs. In\-office expectations Office\-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in\-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in\-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible. Pay and benefits The annual salary range for this role in the primary location is €106,400 \- €159,600\. This range may change if you are hired in another location. For sales roles, the range provided is the role’s On Target Earnings (“OTE”) range, meaning that the range includes both the sales commissions/sales bonuses target and annual base salary for the role. This salary range may be inclusive of several career levels at Stripe and will be narrowed during the interview process based on a number of factors, including the candidate’s experience, qualifications, and specific location. Applicants interested in this role and who are not located in the primary location may request the annual salary range for their location during the interview process. Specific benefits and details about what compensation is included in the salary range listed above will vary depending on the applicant’s location and can be discussed in more detail during the interview process. Benefits/additional compensation for this role may include: equity, company bonus or sales commissions/bonuses; retirement plans; health benefits; and wellness stipends.

title: GECX AI Forward Deployed Engineer I, Google Cloud
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, a related field, or equivalent practical experience. * Experience architecting AI systems on cloud platforms (e.g., Google Cloud Platform (GCP)). * Experience deploying resources via Terraform to automate the setup of agents, functions, or networking. * Experience building full\-stack applications that interact with enterprise IT infrastructures and developing external customer projects. * 2 years of experience with software development in one or more programming languages (e.g., Python). **Preferred qualifications:** * Master’s degree or PhD in AI, Computer Science, or a related technical field. * Experience implementing multi\-agent systems using frameworks like ReAct and self\-reflection. * Experience debugging Agent logic and optimizing tool selection, including tracing conversation IDs across microservices to identify and resolve failures in real\-time. * Experience connecting agents to enterprise knowledge bases and optimizing retrieval\-augmented generation (RAG) chunking to prevent hallucinations. * Ability to communicate in English and French or German fluently to support client relationship management in these regions. * Track record of troubleshooting live, high\-traffic systems during critical windows. **About the job** As a Gemini Enterprise Customer Experience (GECX) Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) in Applied AI, you are the "Agent Engineer" and the primary driver for our customers' most critical AI initiatives. You will take initial conversational prototypes and transform them into production\-ready solutions, owning the end\-to\-end engineering life\-cycle, including the transition from "Art of the Possible" to real\-world business value and scalable, secure AI systems. This is a high\-travel, high\-impact role focused on leading technical delivery for Conversational AI pilots and establishing the first Customer User Journeys (CUJs) for our largest customers at their sites. This role requires an understanding of software engineering, machine learning operations, and cloud infrastructure.It's an exciting time to join Google Cloud’s Go\-To\-Market team, leading the AI revolution for businesses worldwide. You’ll excel by leveraging Google's brand credibility—a legacy built on inventing foundational technologies and proven at scale. We’ll provide you with the world's most advanced AI portfolio, including frontier Gemini models, and the complete Vertex AI platform, helping you to solve business problems. We’re a collaborative culture providing direct access to DeepMind's engineering and research minds, empowering you to solve customer challenges. Join us to be the catalyst for our mission, drive customer success, and define the new cloud era—the market is yours. Individual pay is determined by factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Ireland: €100000 \- €103000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Serve as the lead developer for conversational artificial intelligence (AI) and customer experience (CX) applications, transitioning from rapid prototypes to production\-grade agentic workflows. * Architect and code conversational flows that are not just functional, but optimized for the "connective tissue" between Google’s Conversational AI products and customers’ live infrastructure, including APIs, legacy data silos, and security perimeters. * Build high\-performance evaluation (Eval) pipelines and observability frameworks to optimize agentic workloads, focusing on reasoning loops, tool selection, and reducing latency while maintaining production\-grade security and networking. * Identify repeatable field patterns and technical "friction points" in Google’s AAI stack, converting them into reusable modules or product feature requests for Engineering teams. * Co\-build with customer engineering teams to instill Google\-grade development best practices, ensuring long\-term project success and high end\-user adoption. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: General Operative
description: Production **Job Purpose** To support the business in achieving its daily targets **Reports To** Shift Supervisor **Key Responsibilities** Responsibilities associated with this role include, but are not limited to: * Meet daily production targets on output, waste, yield, efficiency, start\-up time by working as part of a team * Accurately record all production data on the relevant sheets provided at set time frequencies (such as downtime, waste, process sheets, non\-conformances etc.) * Ensure that all quality tests are completed as per protocols and all traceability details are carried out in line with systems requirements * Control and responsibility of all items within the area such as production tools, probes, machine parts in line with metal procedures and other such foreign matter procedures in place. Replacing when required and reporting of items if misplaced * Carry out all tasks to ensure that food safety requirements are achieved * Maintain the work area and machines in a tidy / safe / hygienic manner * Operate all equipment in accordance with training provided and approved SOP * Follow all hygiene and safety procedures * Offer suggestions / new initiatives to a changing / developing process * Work within the quality standards as set out by the business * Ensure all products are produced and packed to company / customer specification and on the ‘get it right first time’ motto * Inform line manager of any maintenance issues identified * Communicate with other members of staff to ensure continuous supply of materials to the production line to ensure uninterrupted stoppages to the process * Be flexible to ensure all orders are met, e.g., in the event of a breakdown occurring on the line **Skills / Qualifications / Experience** * Good interpersonal and communication skills * Excellent understanding of food safety (HACCP) and Health \& Safety processes * High level of accuracy and attention to detail * At least 6 months in a FMCG environment * Ability to work in a team environment * Shows flexibility in all aspects of the role * Understands the business and what needs to get done to help the business to be successful * Is proactive and thinks ahead * Excellent written and verbal English Note: As with all positions, due to the dynamic nature of Arrow Group business, key responsibilities will evolve and change over time. Some travel will be required. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate’s eligibility to work in Ireland Apply Now

title: General Operative – Installation & Civils Support
description: Electro Automation is expanding, and we're looking for a dependable, hands\-on General Operative to join our installation team. You'll be right there on site helping our technicians install automatic doors, gates, parking systems, and all the civils work that goes with them. ### **Your** **Responsibilities** Assist with the installation of automatic doors, gates, barriers, and parking systems. Carry out civils tasks including groundworks, ducting, cutting induction loops, and cable route preparation. Support technicians and electricians throughout the installation process. Keep sites safe, tidy, and ready for the next stage of work. Work across a variety of commercial, industrial, and residential locations. Follow all health and safety procedures on active construction sites. Role Snapshot Department Installation Experience Entry Level Type Full\-time Location Dublin ### **Your Areas of** **Knowledge \& Expertise** Practical, hands\-on approach and a willingness to get stuck in. Comfort working outdoors and on active construction or commercial sites. Physical capability and strong awareness of site safety practices. Full clean driving licence. A positive attitude and genuine team\-player mentality. ### **Desirable** **Skills** Previous experience in civils, groundwork, construction labouring, or general site work. Familiarity with induction loops, ducting, or cable containment. Safe Pass or Manual Handling certification. Prior exposure to automation, gates, barriers, or access control installations. ### **Working Hours \& Package** Installations progressed on schedule thanks to your reliable, proactive support. Sites consistently left safe, clean, and ready for the next phase. Technicians and engineers can rely on you to anticipate needs and work without constant direction. Growing confidence and skill base, with a clear path toward a more specialised role. ### **Ready to Take Your Career to the** **Next Level?** At Electro Automation, you'll join a welcoming and friendly team that values your expertise, supports your growth, and allows you to work on industry\-leading projects that make a real impact. #### **Jenny Dowd** Group Recruitment Business Partner

title: General Operator
description: We are currently seeking to recruit an enthusiastic **General Operator** to join the Biofuel's Team based in Nobber. This is a full time role will work as part of a team to run the biodiesel process and intake/dispatch of feedstocks and biodiesel. The shift range will vary and will be scheduled in advance, normally 9 or 12 hours. College Group was founded in 1989 and currently operates from three state\-of\-the\-art sites; Nobber in County Meath and Monnery in County Cavan, with Newry in County Down. College Biofuels is a renewable energy company producing sustainable, environmentally friendly fuel for the transport and heating sectors. **Key Responsibilities:** * Running and monitoring process using PLC system in the control room * Following production plan * Taking samples to lab for analysis * Clearing lines/General house\-keeping * Dispatching biodiesel in a timely manner, Off\-loading feedstock to ensure supply available for production * Use of shunter \- training will be provided. **Skills required:** * Must be self\-motivated and have the ability to work in a team and/or on own initiative, * Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills, with ability to meet targets and project deadlines, * Ability to interact and communicate effectively with a wide range of people, * Active / physical work * Positive attitude \- reliable and strong work ethic.

title: Generator Fitter - Dublin
description: **About the Role** If you’re a skilled, hands\-on, and experienced Generator Fitter with an eye for detail, a proactive attitude, and a passion for problem\-solving, this role is for you! We’re looking for an engineer who enjoys a mix of workshop\-based and field work — someone who can maintain, service, and repair generators, tanks, cables, BSUs, and distribution boards both in our depots and out at our customers sites.
