title: IT Support Engineer - Contractor (L2)
description: Nostra is a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP) in Ireland, dedicated to delivering advanced IT solutions that enable businesses to build reliable, secure, and scalable IT foundations. With a commitment to continuous improvement, we serve a diverse range of industries, providing exceptional services that empower our clients to focus on their core business goals. This exciting contractor (day rate) position will be based on\-site for initially 12 months with the wider ICT support and Infrastructure team with one of our public sector customers in Dublin City Centre. **Responsibilities:** * Providing end\-user support for PC and peripheral hardware and software issues. * Setting up new hardware for end users. * Installation of application software packages, drivers etc. * Installing and configuring desktop software operating systems and applications. * Deploying PC/Laptop images. Installing software patches. * Record, Update and Track all user issues/requests and correspondence in ticketing system. * Troubleshooting user connectivity and peripheral issues e.g. network problems, including Wi\-Fi connectivity, printing/plotting, password issues, rights issues etc. * Dealing with user issues face to face at the walk\-up Kiosk, when required. * Providing support to the large mobile phone estate. **Requirements:** * Minimum 3 years’ experience in Helpdesk remote support \& onsite support environment. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in setting up, configuring and managing PCs, notebooks, peripherals (e.g. scanners) in a MS Windows 11 environment. * Expert knowledge of PC imaging technologies. * Strong understanding of Microsoft Active Directory. * Strong working experience with O365\. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in installing software packages. * Support experience in desktop applications such as email, outlook, browsers, MS office etc. * Strong knowledge and hands\-on experience in troubleshooting network/WIFI connectivity issues including physical networking (e.g. cabling and protocols such as Ethernet and TCP/IP. * Strong working knowledge and hands\-on experience in supporting network printers/plotters in Managed Print Service (MPS) environment. * Working Knowledge of Client based Video Conferencing. * Working knowledge of Baramundi unified endpoint management system or similar. * Strong experience supporting remote users using remote management software such as Dameware/TeamViewer or similar. * Experience supporting remote user Single Sign On and Two Factor Authentication. * Certifications CompTIA A\+, CompTIA Networking\+, MCSA, O365 are a distinct advantage. At Nostra we value our people. We are a passionate team committed to doing our best, our values are trust, accountability, expertise, people, integrity, and empathy. These values form a central part of our recruitment process. Nostra is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.

title: IT Support Engineer - Level 2
description: **PFH Technology are currently looking for an experienced L2 IT Support Engineer to join our team in Dublin.** **Objective:** Provide hands on end user support, administration, installation and issue resolution onsite for our client. **Contract:** 12\-month contract (with expected extensions) **Location** : Fully onsite, Dublin 1 **Prerequisites:** ITIL V4, Microsoft Certifications including MD 102 **Skills/Requirements** **1\. Technical Skills** * **Operating Systems:** Advanced knowledge of various operating systems like Windows, macOS, and Linux. * Installing, configuring, and troubleshooting OS\-related issues. * Knowledge of Active Directory (AD), Group Policies, and domain\-related issues (for Windows environments). * **Network Troubleshooting:** * In\-depth understanding of networking concepts such as DNS, DHCP, TCP/IP, subnetting, VLANs, and firewalls. * Diagnosing and resolving issues related to routers, switches, firewalls, and VPNs. * Monitoring and maintaining network performance. * **Hardware and Software Troubleshooting:** * Ability to diagnose hardware failures (laptops, desktops, servers). * Knowledge of software deployment, updates, and patch management. * **Virtualization:** Familiarity with virtualization platforms like VMware, Hyper\-V, or VirtualBox. * **Cloud Technologies:** Understanding cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud) and managing cloud services like virtual machines, storage, and cloud backups. * **Scripting and Automation:** Basic scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Bash, Python) for automating routine tasks and troubleshooting complex issues. **2\. Advanced Troubleshooting and Problem Solving** * **Root Cause Analysis:** Ability to perform deep dives into issues to identify root causes rather than simply resolving symptoms. * **Log Analysis:** Reading and interpreting system and network logs to diagnose and resolve technical issues. * **System Performance Tuning:** Diagnosing performance bottlenecks in hardware, applications, and networks, and suggesting improvements. * **Incident Management:** Following standard ITIL (Information Technology Infrastructure Library) practices for incident escalation, documentation, and resolution. **3\. Communication Skills** * **Customer Service:** Communicating complex technical information in a user\-friendly way to non\-technical staff or clients. * **Collaboration:** Working effectively with Level 1 (frontline) support teams, and escalating issues to Level 3 or specialized support teams when necessary. * **Documentation:** Creating and updating technical documentation, including knowledge base articles and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * **Ticket Management:** Using ticketing systems (e.g., Jira, ServiceNow, Zendesk) to manage and resolve technical support requests. **4\. Security Awareness** * **Cybersecurity Knowledge:** Awareness of security best practices, threat mitigation, and basic concepts like encryption, VPNs, multi\-factor authentication (MFA), and firewalls. * **Endpoint Security:** Ensuring that workstations, laptops, and mobile devices are secured against potential threats. * **Backup and Disaster Recovery:** Familiarity with backup solutions, disaster recovery planning, and ensuring data integrity. **5\. Database Management** * **Basic SQL Knowledge:** Understanding of databases and SQL queries for troubleshooting database\-related issues or working with database administrators (DBAs). * **Backup and Restore:** Ability to perform basic database backups and restore operations in case of failure. **6\. Project Support** * **Support for IT Projects:** Participating in IT projects such as software rollouts, infrastructure upgrades, or migrations. * **Change Management:** Understanding change control processes and ensuring that system changes are well\-documented and implemented according to organizational policies. **7\. Remote Support Tools** * **Remote Troubleshooting:** Proficiency with tools like TeamViewer, AnyDesk, or Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) for assisting users remotely. * **Monitoring Tools:** Familiarity with network and system monitoring tools (e.g., Nagios, SolarWinds, PRTG) to proactively identify issues. **8\. Time Management and Prioritization** * **Task Prioritization:** Ability to prioritize tasks based on the severity and impact of technical issues. * **Multitasking:** Managing multiple incidents or service requests at the same time while ensuring timely resolution. **9\. Vendor and Third\-Party Support** * **Vendor Management:** Liaising with third\-party vendors for escalated technical issues related to specific hardware or software products. * **Warranty and RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization) Management:** Handling warranty claims and equipment replacements through vendor support. **10\. Certifications:** **Essential** * **Microsoft Certifications:** (e.g., Microsoft Certified: Azure Administrator, Microsoft 365 Certified). * **ITIL Foundation V4:** For understanding IT service management (ITSM) processes. **Optional but Beneficial** * **CompTIA Network\+, CompTIA Security\+** for networking and security fundamentals. * **Cisco Certifications:** (e.g., CCNA for networking fundamentals). **Education** * Third Level Education Qualification is preferable. PFH Technology, owned by Ricoh – a Japanese based global ICT company, is a premier provider of end\-to\-end ICT solutions and a managed services portfolio scaling from SMEs to large Enterprise organisations. We have unrivalled vendor relationships. We can procure, design, deploy and support all your ICT needs. Our ISO certified Custodian™ Cloud Services and Custodian™ Managed Services provide the technology and expertise to mitigate risk and reduce your costs immediately. We have a nationwide network of over 750 dedicated professionals, ready to meet your ICT needs, with offices in Dublin, Cork and Galway.

title: Identity & Access Management Engineer
description: **Position Overview:** We are seeking an experienced Senior Identity \& Access Management (IAM) Engineer for **5 days per week on\-site** in a South Dublin office for a high\-impact, enterprise\-level cloud transformation project in Ireland. This role requires a highly technical professional with extensive expertise in Ping Identity products, modern Single Sign\-On (SSO) protocols, and multi\-cloud security architectures. The successful candidate will be responsible for defining deployment pipelines, implementing robust test suites, and executing custom Ping policies within a global, large\-scale operational environment. **Key Responsibilities:** * **Architecture \& Deployment:** Contribute to story refinement, define system requirements, and utilize network, virtualization, and data center technologies to execute secure deployment strategies. * **Identity Synchronization:** Design custom Ping policies and federate cloud applications with PingFederate. Implement identity synchronization to Cloud IAM utilizing PingIDM and SCIM. * **Pipeline Management:** Define, build, and improve deployment pipelines and processes supporting system transformation, quality controls, dependency, and workload management. * **Testing \& Triage:** Implement and execute existing test suites (integration, regression, and performance), analyze test reports, and triage underlying root causes. * **Collaboration \& Documentation:** Work across development teams to ensure delivery of system requirements. Document and communicate critical information for deployment, maintenance, and support. **Required Experience and Qualifications:** * **Professional Experience:** 7\+ years of dedicated experience in Information Security, with a primary focus on Identity and Access Management. * **SSO \& Protocols:** Deep hands\-on experience with industry\-standard SSO technologies and protocols including OAuth, OpenID Connect (OIDC), FIDO, SCIM, LDAP, and SAML. * **Ping Identity Suite:** Proven expertise in Identity and Authentication solutions, specifically PingFederate, PingAccess, and PingIDM. * **Cloud Architecture:** Strong experience developing and integrating Cloud IAM solutions across AWS, GCP, and Azure environments. * **Scripting \& Development:** Proficient in utilizing various programming or scripting languages, such as Java/J2EE, Python, PowerShell, Shell scripting, Perl, or JavaScript. * **Directory Services:** Solid understanding of LDAP, Active Directory services, MFA, Risk\-based authentication, and Privileged Access Management (PAM). * **Environment Knowledge:** Familiarity with Linux, Windows, Tomcat, WebLogic, and IBM WebSphere environments, alongside standard API design concepts and RESTful services.

title: Installer
description: Due to continued growth, we are seeking skilled and motivated **Architectural Metalwork Installers** and **Pergola Installers** to join our expanding team. **Role Summary** The successful candidate will be responsible for the safe, accurate, and high\-quality installation across a variety of commercial and residential sites. The role requires strong technical ability, attention to detail, and the professionalism to work independently or as part of a site team while meeting agreed timelines and company standards. **Key Responsibilities** * Read, interpret, and work from technical drawings, plans, specifications, and job instructions. * Install balustrade systems (glass, aluminium, steel or composite) and external/internal cladding systems to a high standard. * Ensure all works are completed safely and in accordance with company procedures, industry standards, and Health \& Safety regulations. * Carry out site preparation, setting out, fixing, alignment, and finishing works with precision and attention to detail. * Communicate effectively with supervisors, colleagues, and clients, raising any site issues, risks, or delays promptly. * Maintain company tools, equipment, and vehicles, reporting defects, damage, or servicing requirements as required. * Complete job cards, reports, photos, and documentation using company scheduling and job management software. * Work flexibly across multiple sites and locations nationwide, including occasional evening or weekend work when required. **Skills \& Experience Required** * Minimum 2 years’ on\-site installation experience, ideally within Pergola’s, balustrade, cladding, façade, architectural metalwork, or similar construction\-related trades. * Strong understanding of fixings, tolerances, levels, and finishes. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and the ability to adapt to site conditions. * High level of workmanship, reliability, and professionalism. * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a crew. * Confident using mobile apps or software for job cards, scheduling, and reporting. * Full, valid Irish or EU driving licence essential. **Health \& Safety \& Qualifications** * Current Safe Pass (essential). * MEWP / Working at Heights certification (desirable). * Manual Handling certification (desirable). * Strong knowledge of Workplace Health \& Safety practices. * First Aid certification is an advantage. **Right to Work** * EU/EEA nationals must provide proof of right to work in Ireland. * Non\-EU/EEA nationals must hold current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. **Company Culture \& What We Offer** Connect Install Ltd is a young, dynamic, and ambitious company that values teamwork, communication, and continuous improvement. We foster a supportive environment where knowledge is shared, and individuals are encouraged to grow alongside the business. We offer: * Competitive salary, ROP between €18\-€24 per hour dependant on experience * Additional annual leave above statutory entitlement * Company Pension Scheme * Uniform allowance * Employee incentives * Ongoing training and upskilling opportunities * Career progression and increased benefits with demonstrated skills and responsibility * Exposure to a wide variety of high\-quality projects nationwide **Contract Details** Full\-Time Contract of Employment with normal working hours Monday to Friday OOHW at time/half 6\-month probation period applies to all contract types **Ready to Apply?** Send your CV to **careers@connectinstall.ie** *All applications are handled confidentially.* Pay: €35,000\.00\-€48,672\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee assistance program Work Location: In person

title: Junior Chef

We know that our talented chefs are the key to delivering exceptional service and tasteful experiences for our clients, customers, and employees. We are looking for a reliable and enthusiastic Kitchen Assistant / Junior Chef to join our busy workplace hospitality operation. This is an excellent opportunity for someone with kitchen experience who is looking for a great work\-life balance while continuing to develop their culinary skills. This role would suit a Kitchen Assistant looking to progress, or a Junior Chef seeking a stable role with excellent work\-life balance and opportunities to grow. **What you'll do:** * Cook up mouth watering menu items, with a passion for perfection * Ensure the highest standards of food hygiene and health and safety are maintained * Contribute to a positive and innovative kitchen culture * Bring your ideas to the table and cook up new techniques **What We're Looking For:** * Previous kitchen experience is essential · * Comfortable working independently and managing your workload * Positive attitude with a willingness to learn and develop * + Reliable, punctual, and professional * Good attention to detail and pride in producing quality food **Why join The Good Eating Company?** **Unlike traditional hospitality roles, this position offers the opportunity to work in a modern, professional workplace hospitality environment, giving you the chance to continue your passion for food while enjoying your evenings and weekends free. The Good Eating Company was cooked up with passion around a kitchen table in the UK in 1999\. We started with one big dream — to bring restaurant\-quality food to corporate dining — and have been championing flavour and food creativity ever since. * The Good Eating Company wholeheartedly supports the principle of equal opportunities in employment and opposes all forms of unlawful or unfair discrimination on the grounds of age, disability, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, race, gender, marital status or sexual orientation.

description: We are a rapidly growing software provider and progressive business that offers a variety of digital banking solutions for its customers and organisations all over the world. We are now looking for a **Junior Sales Specialist** to join our expanding sales function, supporting different product verticals within the Financial Services ecosystem. **Responsibilities:** * Develop a strong understanding of sales trends and developments within the Financial Services and Payments industry. * Establish, develop, and maintain positive business relationships with prospective and existing clients. * Present, promote, and sell products and services using structured and persuasive sales approaches. * Actively reach out to customer leads through various channels. * Manage and resolve client queries and issues to maximise satisfaction. * Achieve agreed sales targets and outcomes within set timelines. * Coordinate sales activities with internal teams and relevant departments. * Provide regular reports on customer needs, market feedback, competitive activity, and sales performance. * Support the preparation of sales materials and PowerPoint presentations. * Organise and schedule meetings in line with business priorities. **Team \& Product Focus:** Depending on business needs, the role may support different sales teams, with a focus on one of the following areas: * Selling multiple payment solutions, including at least five different payment methods (e.g. cards, local payment methods, alternative payments, digital wallets, etc.), or * Selling credit card products, with the ability to manage and close sales for a minimum of five card\-based solutions **Requirements:** * Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Sales, or a related field. * Experience or strong interest in Finance, Fintech, or Payment industries. * Understanding of sales processes and customer acquisition fundamentals. * Excellent communication, presentation, and negotiation skills. * Strong time management and organisational abilities. * Analytical mindset with problem\-solving skills. * Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with wider teams.

title: Junior Security Analyst, Threat Detection Operations
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience. * 2 years of experience with security assessments or security design reviews or threat modeling. * Experience with host, network, and cloud security principles and concepts (e.g., common attacker tactics, tools, and techniques (TTPs)). **Preferred qualifications:** * Certification in Security\+ or similar Cyber Security/Incident Response. * Experience analyzing, triaging, and remediating common information security incidents. * Experience with automation via coding. * Ability to work separately, prioritize, and multitask. * Excellent problem\-solving, investigative, and written and verbal communication skills. * Excellent log/data analysis skill analysing a large set of data using sql or similar query language. **About the job** In this role, you will develop and maintain the signals, tools, and infrastructure that help Google evolve to match attackers. Ireland: €68000 \- €70000 (EUR) \+ 15% bonus target \+ equity \+ benefits **Responsibilities** Learn more about benefits at Google . * Analyze, investigate, and respond to security alerts. * Research new risk hunting techniques to identify/discover security risks. * Automate workflows and develop solutions to reduce operational toil. * Ensure compliance with legal mandates and internal security and privacy policies. * Manage response efforts for low severity incidents. title: KITCHEN TEAM MEMBER
description: We believe eating well should never mean sacrificing taste. Our kitchen team are at the heart of what we do, passionate about food, obsessed with flavour, and focused on delivering fresh, delicious dishes every day. This is a role for someone who loves being in the kitchen, has great attention to detail, and wants to grow their skills in a fast\-paced environment. **Reporting to:** General Manager **Contract:** Full\-time \& Part\-time available **Pay:** €14\.50–€15\.25 per hour depending on experience and role ****What You’ll Be Doing**** * Food Safety \& Compliance – Follow all food safety protocols and ensure everything we serve meets the highest safety standards. * Hygiene Hero – Keep the kitchen spotless and ensure equipment is always sanitised and safe to use. * Allergen Alert – Understand allergens and help ensure every dish is safely prepared for all customers. * Recipe Rundown – Work with prep sheets and help keep the kitchen running smoothly with accurate, consistent prep. * Flavour Focus – Care deeply about taste and presentation. No problem. * Attention to Detail – From portion sizes to plating, you notice the little things that make a big difference. * Good Level of English – You can follow instructions, communicate with your team, and understand food safety requirements. * Reliable \& Organised – You keep things clean, follow checklists, and stay on top of your prep. * Curious \& Willing to Learn – You’re excited to build your skills and grow with the team. title: MHPSS Cultural Mediation Associate (Georgian)
description: Applications are welcome from internal and external candidates. For all IOM vacancies, applications from qualified and eligible first\-tier candidates are considered before those of qualified and eligible second\-tier candidates in the selection process. For the purpose of this vacancy, internal candidates are considered first\-tier candidates. Context IOM Ireland has been operating in Ireland since 2001, currently in following areas of migration management: Assisted Voluntary Return and Reintegration, Resettlement, Family Reunification, Counter\-Trafficking, Protection, Integration Support to Beneficiaries of Temporary Protection, Integrated Immigration Governance and Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS). IOM provides mental health and psychosocial support to international protection seekers residing in the Citywest accommodation facility with the aim to improve their psychosocial well\-being and to strengthen their overall resilience. Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission in Ireland, the Head of MHPSS Unit, the Cultural Mediation and Community Engagement Coordinator and direct supervision of Senior Cultural Mediation Associate, the incumbent will support the implementation of activities related to community mobilization and pertinent activities for international protection applicants hosted in Citywest accommodation centre. * RESPONSIBILITIES Provide cultural mediation and interpretation support by facilitating communication between service providers and migrants (from English into different migrants’ mother tongue and vice versa), while always respecting the principles of neutrality, accuracy, impartiality, confidentiality. * Provide verbal interpretation support to the International Protection Accommodation Services (IPAS) staff in Citywest to support day\-to\-day communication with beneficiaries from English into different migrants’ languages. * Assist through interpretation during the collection of routine information and data and case identification and referrals. * Support the beneficiaries in contacting service providers within Citywest by providing interpretation and mediation support in an impartial and neutral manner. * Support the identification of vulnerabilities from the initial reception into the centre and throughout the continued communication with beneficiaries and refer them to the Senior Case Management Associate for case management. * Support IOM partners in the implementation of community engagement and awareness\-raising activities by assisting with logistics, interpretation, and beneficiary outreach. * Conduct outreach activities to inform beneficiaries about available MHPSS services and scheduled activities, in coordination with MHPSS team. * Provide interpretation assistance to mental health professionals during scheduled group and individual counselling sessions, focus group discussions and psycho\-education activities, including remote interpretation (phone or online), when required. * Prepare routine documentation related to the project and submit basic reports to the Project Management Team on activities performed in Citywest. * Any other related task, as required. QUALIFICATIONS Education * High school diploma with five years of relevant experience; or, * Bachelor’s degree in Communication, Social Sciences, International relations or related fields from an accredited academic institution with at least 3 years of relevant professional experience. Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database . Experience * Completion of trainings in cultural mediation for mental health settings, and competence in the MHPSS specialized vocabulary would be an asset. * Experience in cultural mediation in the framework of migrants’ assistance. * Previous experience with international organizations, NGOs, EU Agencies and/or Irish law enforcements or Government’s Departments would be an asset. Skills * Ability to work with migrants and asylum seekers. * Interpersonal skills (i.e. empathy and emotional resilience) * Intercultural competence and communication skills. * Culture\-specific knowledge. * Capacity to prevent and mitigate misunderstandings due to language barriers by facilitating communication in an objective and impartial manner. * Basic understanding in interpersonal communication and sensitivity in addressing the asylum\-seekers’ claims. * Knowledge of the procedures related to the asylum application in Ireland. * Ability to prepare clear and concise routine reports and basic written summaries. * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Office. Languages * For this position, fluency in English is required (oral and written). * Fluency or mother tongue in Georgian is required IOM’s official languages are English, French and Spanish. Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments. **Required Competencies** Competencies will be assessed during the selection process. Values \- all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these three values: * Inclusion and respect for diversity: Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion. * Integrity and transparency: Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. * Professionalism: Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. * Courage: Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance. * Empathy: Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated. Core Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) * Teamwork: Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. * Delivering results: Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. * Managing and sharing knowledge: Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. * Accountability: Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work. * Communication: Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way. Managerial Competencies – behavioural indicators (Level 1\) (applicable only if position is with direct reports) * Leadership: Provides a clear sense of direction, leads by example and demonstrates the ability to carry out the Organization’s vision. Assists others to realize and develop their leadership and professional potential. * Empowering others: Creates an enabling environment where staff can contribute their best and develop their potential. * Building Trust: Promotes shared values and creates an atmosphere of trust and honesty. * Strategic thinking and vision: Works strategically to realize the Organization’s goals and communicates a clear strategic direction. * Humility: Leads with humility and shows openness to acknowledging own shortcomings. **Notes*** Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. * This selection process may be used to staff similar positions in various duty stations. Recommended candidates will remain eligible to be appointed in a similar position for a period of 24 months. * This post is subject to local recruitment. Only those holding a valid residence and work permit for the country where this position is based will be eligible for consideration. * Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, verification of residency, visa, and authorizations by the concerned Government, where applicable. * IOM has a zero\-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. * IOM does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process (application, interview, processing, training or other fee). IOM does not request any information related to bank accounts. * IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the IOM online recruitment system. The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. * A s the right to live and work in Ireland is required, the contract issued may be shorter than the period mentioned in the advertisement, in order to align with expiry date of the work permit. For further information and other job postings, you are welcome to visit our website: IOM Careers and Job Vacancies

title: Microbiology Analyst
description: **Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company, providing a unique range of analytical testing services to clients across multiple industries, to make life and our environment safer, healthier and more sustainable. From the food you eat, to the water you drink, to the medicines you rely on, Eurofins laboratories work with the biggest companies in the world to ensure the products they supply are safe, their ingredients are authentic, and labelling is accurate.** **Eurofins is dedicated to delivering testing services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, and to its corporate responsibility to protect the environment and ensure diversity, equity, and inclusion across the entire network of Eurofins companies.** **Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is a global, award\-winning insourcing solution. We place our skilled team directly at the client site, managing operations seamlessly and eliminating complexities. We ensure a strategic, long\-term service commitment that delivers value directly at the client’ site, utilising their facilities, equipment, and methodologies, while maintaining the same service, expertise, and cGMP compliance available at the Eurofins facility. If you're an employee seeking an exceptional career journey, Eurofins PSS Insourcing Solutions® is your gateway to a future where innovation, collaboration, and success converge. Join us in a world where excellence knows no boundaries!** **We’re proud to be an armed forces\-friendly place to work and welcome applications from reservists, armed forces veterans (including the wounded, injured and sick), as well as cadet instructors, and military spouses and partners.** **If you want to continue to help make our world a safer and healthier place, then apply for the role below and become a part of the extraordinary Eurofins network.** **Job Description** **Unfortunately, we are unable to offer visa sponsorship for this role.** **This is a full time, 12 Month Fixed Term Contract position. 39 hours per week, Working Monday \- Thursday 08:00 – 16:30 \& Friday 08:00 – 15:30\.** As a Microbiology Analyst you will work in a cross functional team to support the Microbiology Quality Control testing function, ensuring compliance with cGMP. **Job Responsibilities that you be covering:** * You will perform a broad range of microbiology testing including bioburden, endotoxin, microbial ID, Biological Indicator testing, BacT/ALERT, sterility checks, and growth‑promotion studies, while carrying out routine laboratory sampling, plate reading, data review, and trending. * You will conduct comprehensive environmental monitoring of cleanroom areas, including viable and non‑viable particulate monitoring and water and gas system monitoring, ensuring uniformity, reproducibility, and GMP‑compliant practices. * You will support and lead quality investigations related to deviations, prepare and revise protocols, reports, and SOPs, and review analytical data for GMP compliance with strong documentation accuracy. * You will collaborate closely with the client team, providing daily updates, maintaining strong working relationships, delivering excellent customer service, and ensuring all decisions align with GMP standards and customer expectations. * You will contribute to training, cross‑coverage, and continuous improvement by developing solutions to problems, identifying opportunities to enhance quality and service, updating documentation, and keeping training records current. * You will maintain a clean, safe, and audit‑ready laboratory environment, operate flexibly with shifting priorities, and consistently demonstrate strong organisation, communication, and problem‑solving skills while working both independently and as part of a team. **Qualifications** **What skills, qualifications and experiences you will hold as a Microbiology Analyst:** * You will hold a degree in Microbiology or a related discipline, along with at least one year of experience in a pharmaceutical regulatory environment. * You will bring hands‑on environmental monitoring experience, strong knowledge of cGMP requirements, and the ability to work independently while meeting established timelines. * You will have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to form strong working relationships and avoid misunderstandings in technical exchanges. * You will have a customer‑focused mindset, demonstrating ownership for speed, quality, and accuracy, along with a passion for quality and high service standards. * You will bring adaptability, resilience under pressure, and the ability to quickly learn new processes, switch priorities, and contribute effectively within a dynamic, growing team. * You will bring strong organisation, accuracy, and documentation skills, along with the capability to solve problems, work with limited supervision, and support continuous improvement. ****Additional Information**** At Eurofins we are growing, innovating and always learning. We celebrate the achievements of our employees through annual long service awards, recognise our colleagues special life events. **As a Eurofins employee you will benefit from:** * We offer exceptional opportunities for career growth, within a friendly and supportive team. As well as a competitive salary, pension and associated benefits you will enjoy an unrivalled working environment, including an active social calendar and subsidised canteen, all while working in a start of the art client facility\- a leading biotechnology company * Fantastic developmental opportunities and career progression pathways such as leadership, training, compliance and Subject Media Expert. * Full Benefits Package including:\- + Pension + Private Medical Insurance + Income protection and life assurance + Annual discretionary bonus + Birthday Holiday entitlement * Exciting Eurofins Incentives: Employee Activity Committee (EAC), Equality Driving Excellence (EDE), significant Reward and Recognition programme Opportunity to benefit from Eurofins Operational Development training platform, plus potential support for part\-time training (relevant to the position) * Full use of the canteen facilities and barista on the client site * Free parking onsite and onsite electric charging * Additional annual leave days with continued service * The opportunity to work on life saving products **What Happens Next** Our people are the backbone of what we do, so it's incredibly important we find the right individuals to join us. As a potential new recruit you'll be invited to meet the team in the form of an assessment centre or a staged interview process dependent on the role and it's requirements, this will give you the opportunity to see what working for Eurofins is really like and enable us to get you know your key skills and strengths. **Your data** As part of any recruitment process, the company collects and processes personal data relating to job applicants. The company is committed to being transparent about how it collects and uses that data and to meeting its data protection obligations. We embrace diversity! Eurofins network of companies believe in strength and innovation through diversity, being an Equal Opportunity Employer. We prohibit discrimination against employees or applications based on gender identity and/or expression, race, nationality, age, religion, sexual orientation, disability, and everything else that makes employees of Eurofins companies unique. **For more information about Eurofins PSS® please visit our** **website** **and watch a message below from our Global Head, Beth Di Paolo.**

We have a **new opportunity** for an enthusiastic and driven **Mobile Generator Fitter** to join our team in **Portlaoise**. **In this role you will be:** * Performing servicing and repairs on rental equipment both on\-site and at the depot. * Preparing machinery and equipment for hire, ensuring operational readiness. * Diagnosing and repairing mechanical and electrical faults on generators and pumps. * Conducting testing on generators to verify performance and reliability. * Support depot operations by providing technical expertise and engineering assistance. **What will help you to excel in this role:** * Relevant technical qualification * Previous experience working on generators and pumps. * Good understanding of hydraulic, mechanical and electrical principals * The ability to work independently and under pressure. * Good communication skills **What you can expect from us:** * Competitive salary * Contributory pension scheme with employer contributions up to 6% * HSF – Healthcare * Future development and career opportunities * Profitshare bonus based on business performance **What’s next** If you are interested in joining The Briggs Group, then please click on the apply now button and a member of the team will be in touch. Not sure if this is the right role for you? Please feel free to visit the careers page on our website and send us your CV speculatively for a member of the team to review. If you require any reasonable adjustments to assist you in the interview process, then please contact a member of the Recruitment Team, and inform them prior to attending so we can ensure these are met.

title: Multi Skilled Carpenter - Residential Projects - Full Time
description: Multi Skilled Carpenter \- Residential Renovations (Full Time Work) Location: North Dublin, Ireland Employment Type: Full\-Time Salary: €30 \- €37 hour Working Hours: 8\-5am M\-T; 8\-4pm Friday **About Us** Deeley Construction is a well\-established company specializing in high\-end residential renovations and extensions. Our projects demand precision, initiative, and an ability to take ownership of the work—from breaking ground to final handover. We pride ourselves on our commitment to quality, professionalism, and maintaining a safe, tidy, and respectful work environment. **Role Overview** We are seeking a highly experienced and self\-motivated Multi Skilled Carpenter to support our residential construction projects from start to finish. This is a hands\-on role ideal for someone who brings broad construction experience, strong problem\-solving ability, and a proactive mindset. You must be capable of working independently, making sound decisions on site, and contributing across all phases of the build \- from groundworks to final completion. You’ll be expected to think on your feet, solve problems without fuss, and keep progress moving without needing to be micromanaged. **Key Responsibilities** * Undertake a wide range of construction tasks including demolition, groundworks, steelwork, roofing, structural work, and internal and external finishing. * Own the progress of your tasks, using initiative and practical knowledge to identify solutions and keep momentum on site. * Apply common sense and site experience to get the job done safely and to a high standard \- even when plans evolve. * Read and interpret technical drawings, blueprints, and specifications with precision. * Maintain a clean, organized, and hazard\-free work environment. * Ensure correct and safe use of all tools and machinery. * Liaise with supervisors and colleagues as needed to ensure smooth site operations. * Uphold high standards of workmanship and site conduct at all times. **Requirements** * Minimum 10 years’ experience in residential construction and renovation. * Demonstrated ability to work independently and solve problems on\-site. * Strong experience across key phases of construction including groundworks, structural work, and roofing. * Own tools and reliable transport (must be able to bring tools to site daily). * Safe Pass and Manual Handling certification preferred (or willingness to obtain). * In\-depth knowledge of Irish building codes and site health and safety. * Excellent timekeeping, reliability, and communication skills. * Strong physical fitness and ability to handle the demands of on\-site work. * Strong site initiative \- you understand how a build flows, where problems may arise, and how to handle them calmly and competently. * A reputation for being dependable, tidy, and easy to work with. **Desirable Skills** * Ability to operate small machinery (e.g. excavators, teleporters) is a plus. * Experience with tiling, plaster patching, and remedial works. * Experience setting out small works and ensuring levels and measurements are correct. * Flexible, “can\-do” attitude with a proactive, problem\-solving mindset. **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience and skillset. * A supportive and respectful work environment. * High\-quality residential projects where skilled craftsmanship is valued. * Opportunities for professional growth and development. * A strong team culture focused on safety, quality, and trust. If you’re a highly experienced construction operative who thrives in a role with autonomy, variety, and accountability, this is the position for you. To Apply: Please send your CV and a short cover letter outlining your experience and suitability for the role. References will be requested at interview stage. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €30\.00\-€37\.00 per hour Benefits: * Company events Application question(s): * Do you own your own set of tools? * Do you have your own transport? Experience: * Residential Carpentry : 10 years (preferred) Language: * Fluent english (required) Licence/Certification: * A Driving Licence (required) Location: * North Dublin, County Dublin (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: NSS202588 Radiography Assistant, BreastCheck Dublin
description: **Description** The National Screening Service (NSS) delivers four national population\-based screening programmes – for cervical, breast and bowel screening and for detecting sight\-threatening retinopathy in people with diabetes. Our programmes focus on looking for early signs of disease in healthy people, so that we can: * detect health issues early * help prevent disease * ensure that anyone who does develop the disease being screened for has the best chance of early, more effective treatment * reduce people’s risk of developing a disease or its complications * support people in making informed decisions about their health **Choose Screening:** Together we can make a difference the National Screening Service 5year Strategic Plan outlining our strategic priorities and goals for the years 2023\-2027\. **Our mission:** We deliver population screening programmes that help prevent, reduce the risk of, and assist the recognition of, disease in Ireland. **Our vision:** To work together to save lives and improve people’s health through population screening. The role of the National Screening Service also includes policy, development and advice. **The Opportunity** To assist and support the delivery of client care under the supervision and direction of Radiography Service Manager or designate. The Radiography Assistant post at BreastCheck is broad based and includes assisting in Assessment Clinics and providing support to the Clinical Team to clients who have or fear they may have breast cancer. There is currently **one permanent and wholetime vacancy** available in BreastCheck Eccles Screening Unit, 36 Eccles Street, Dublin 1 A panel will be formed for future permanent and specified purpose vacancies in BreastCheck Merrion Screening Unit, Merrion Road, Dublin 4 **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * The primary role of the Radiography Assistant is to assist, Radiographers \& Radiologists with the general day to day management of client care and associated duties in BreastCheck. * To deliver a quality, safe, efficient and caring radiology and imaging service to clients under the supervision of the Radiography Service Manager or designate. * To assist radiographers, radiologists and the multi\-disciplinary team with all duties pertaining to breast cancer screening, assessment and administration. * The Radiography Assistant is broad based and includes assisting in Assessment Clinics and providing support to the clinical team to clients who have or fear they may have breast cancer. **Please Refer To Job Description For Detailed Eligibilty Criteria** Join our team and we'll provide you with the support you need to deliver and succeed. This is an unmissable opportunity to join a diverse and supportive workplace where staff feel valued. \#ChooseScreening

title: Network Engineer - Contractor (L2)
description: Nostra is a leading Managed Service Provider (MSP) in Ireland, dedicated to delivering advanced IT solutions that enable businesses to build reliable, secure, and scalable IT foundations. With a commitment to continuous improvement, we serve a diverse range of industries, providing exceptional services that empower our clients to focus on their core business goals. This exciting contractor (day rate) position will be based for 12 months on\-site with the wider ICT support and Infrastructure team with one of our public sector customers in Dublin City Centre. You will be responsible for managing and supporting a large, complex WAN environment across multiple locations throughout Ireland, delivered via a range of service providers. The network spans a multi\-vendor infrastructure, including FortiGate SD\-WAN firewalls, Alcatel\-Lucent Omni Switches, and Aruba WiFi solutions. Connectivity across the estate is diverse, incorporating technologies such as MPLS, eFibre, DSL, MSA, 4G/5G, and satellite links. SD\-WAN, primarily based on Fortinet technology, is deployed extensively across the network estate. **Key Responsibilities:** **Project Delivery, Documentation and Reporting:** * Understand the goals of collaborate effectively with internal teams. * Plan, coordinate and track project tasks proactively. * Contribute to project reviews with team. * Maintain detailed and up\-to\-date documentation of network diagrams, configurations, and procedures. * Update the inventory management system. **Network Maintenance and Support:** * Monitor and maintain LAN, WAN and WLAN infrastructure, including switches, routers, access points, SD\-WAN Firewalls and NAC. * Troubleshoot and resolve network issues to minimise downtime. **On\-site Physical Work:** * Cabling: Inspect, install, and maintain network cabling for connectivity and performance. * Racking and Stacking: Install and organise network equipment in racks with proper cable management. **Network Implementation:** * Assist in deploying LAN, WLAN, and NAC solutions as required. * Support Level 3/4 engineers in configuring and installing network hardware and software as required * Facilitate planned maintenance and change management processes. **Support:** * Offer technical support and guidance to the clients users and help desk * Respond to Level 2 and priority helpdesk tickets as required * Record, Update, Track issues/requests and correspondence in Helpdesk ticketing system **Working Conditions:** * Onsite role at clients location. * Standard working hours of 37\.5 hours per week. * May involve occasional after\-hours work to assist in maintenance or upgrades. * Requires the ability to lift and move network equipment when necessary. * Travel at various sites across Ireland will be required at times. **Requirements:** * 2\+ years working experience managing and supporting Network environments. * Full driving license and access to vehicle. * Flexibility to travel will be required across Ireland. * Hands\-on experience in setting up and supporting Network infrastructure. * Hands\-on experience in setting up or managing Wireless networks. * Working knowledge of LAN/WAN protocols such as TCP/IP, DNS/DHCP, Layer2, Switching, Layer3 Routing, Knowledge of OSPF and BGP, OSI Model,NAC (Network Access Control). * Ability to support Wireless Technologies. * Working knowledge of Fortinet (or other) SD WAN technologies. * Working knowledge of Aruba Clearpass, Controllers and Airwave. * Knowledge of Alcatel\-Lucent OmniSwitches (Or Cisco, HP). * Experience with VPN technologies and how they impact various applications. * Experience with other cloud based VC applications. * Experience in dealing with multiple 3rd party WAN providers. * Proven ability to work on their own in an outage situation. * Support experience to users of all levels in busy helpdesk environment. * Hands\-on experience in managing and working in computer rooms. This includes cabling and racks. * Understanding of environmental issues such as air conditioning, UPS, water leak detection * Certifications such as CCNA are a distinctive advantage. At Nostra we value our people. We are a passionate team committed to doing our best, our values are trust, accountability, expertise, people, integrity, and empathy. These values form a central part of our recruitment process. Nostra is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion.

title: Office Administrator
description: We’ve worked hard to build a culture where people feel a genuine sense of belonging, wherever they work in our operations, and whatever their role. Our culture is grounded in our core values and our very strong commitment to delivering every day for our clients and their customers, getting everyone home safely, and caring about your wellbeing. We believe the diverse work we do really matters – whether we are connecting people and businesses to complex fibre networks, supporting large industrial projects and utilities, building modular spaces in which to learn, work or play, or delivering creative events to inspire and entertain. **Responsibilities:** * Adhere to and maintain compliance with all Health and Safety procedures within your work. * Engaging with our customers and clients, assisting them with all queries they have. * Daily and weekly job scheduling * Maintaining the Work Management System * Compiling daily operational reports * Preparation of PowerPoint slides * Engaging with service engineers providing them with support where required * Ad hoc administrative duties **Requirements:** * 1\+ years related experience * Excellent literacy, numeric and IT skills including proficiency in Microsoft Office * Knowledge of Microsoft Excel required * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills * Attention to detail * Experience working in a fast\-paced environment * Decisive, self\-motivated with the ability to represent the company in a professional manner at all times * Innovative, creative and proactive with a solution orientated approach **Benefits:** * Pension plan * Life assurance – 4 times your annual salary * Employee assistance programme * Refer a friend scheme * Maternity pay * Educational assistance * Career progression opportunities * Long Service Awards

title: Operational Risk Analyst
description: A global private markets investment firm is hiring a skilled and ambitious Operational Risk Associate to be based in their Dublin office. Reporting to the Head of Risk, you can expect strong exposure and autonomy in this role. Key Responsibilities: * Support the development and implementation of risk frameworks * Assist with control reviews and identify risk issues * Carry out end\-to\-end operational risk monitoring processes Requirements: * Experience in operational risk within financial services, ideally in asset management, investment banking, or private equity * A solid understanding of investment management and financial products If you’re interested in this opportunity, please apply. If you do not hear back within 72 hours, you can assume you have not been shortlisted on this occasion.

title: Overnight - Residence Experience Associate
description: **Role Summary:** We are seeking a motivated Overnight Resident Experience Associate to join our team at Glass Bottle, a premier Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) property located in Sandymount Strand, Co. Dublin. This is an ideal opportunity for someone looking to progress their career in the growing BTR industry, working for a company that is expanding its footprint in Ireland. The successful candidate, reporting to the Property Manager, will be integral to daily property operations, focusing on resident relations, health and safety, cleanliness and security. This highly visible role will involve regular interaction with residents and visitors helping ensure high standards in resident engagement and satisfaction. We’re seeking candidates with strong experience in customer engagement. This job would ideally suit candidates from hospitality roles like: Security Operative, Night Auditor, Assistant Night Manager / Night Manager. CompassRock has a dedicated and comprehensive training and certification program, and this role will have the potential to progress into more managerial role over time. Some of the training programs include The Property Institute’s Build to Rent course and Managing Safely Qualifications. **Company Overview:** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sector. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across England and Europe. CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, the company values the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs. As CompassRock continues to expand, there will be abundant opportunities for career advancement and professional development. **Key Responsibilities:** **Customer Service \& Resident Relations:** * **Policy and Standards Adherence:** Uphold the company’s customer service standards to deliver high\-quality service and resident satisfaction. * **Issue Resolution:** Address resident complaints and actively troubleshoot problems to take ownership in challenging situations, escalating issues to the Property Manager when necessary. * **Resident Engagement Initiatives**: Support the planning and execution of community\-building activities that foster positive resident relations and engagement. * **Resident Support:** Provide residents with appropriate information throughout the tenancy lifespan, including providing renewal information, ensuring legal requirements are met and answering any questions that might occur during the tenancy lifespan. * **Relationship Building:** Cultivate strong relationships with residents, ensuring professional and prompt communication. Regularly engage with residents and prospective tenants to build strong rapport and trust. * **Leasing Support:** Provide administrative support to the Leasing team, as required. * **Enquiries**: Greet and assist prospective tenants and to qualify and register enquiries and to book viewing appointments at our properties (if required). * **Viewings:** Conduct informative and engaging viewings to prospective tenants being well\-equipped to answer any questions regarding the property (if required). * **Administrative Support:** Working with the leasing team to help maintain accurate and organised records of leases, applications, and tenant correspondence. **Operational Activities:** * **Property Support**: Contribute to maintaining a safe and attractive property for residents. Facilities Management Support: Assist the Property Manager with managing projects, overseeing outsourced services for building maintenance, and ensuring timely, high\-quality completion of tasks. * **Cleanliness**: Complete overnight cleaning of the amenity spaces, ensuring that the property is “resident ready” each day. * **‘Stock’ Coordination:** Collaborate with the property manager and procurement team to ensure adequate stock of goods and services for smooth property operations. Completing stock checks on a monthly basis. * **Inspections:** Conduct / support inventories, move\-in and move\-out inspections, documenting property conditions. * **Administrative Support:** Assist the Property Manager with all other general administrative duties, as required. **Targets and KPIs:** * Work diligently to meet specified targets and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) set by the management team. **Personal Qualities:** * **Attention to Detail:** Demonstrated focus on quality and compliance in previous work or education (desired). * **Proactive and Initiative\-Driven:** Able to foresee potential issues and address them before they escalate (desired). Sharing suggestions on how to improve efficiencies, encourage proactivity and support your colleagues. * **Flexibility:** Ability to adapt to changing circumstances, particularly in a fast\-paced property environment (desired). * **Positive, 'can\-do' Attitude:** A willingness to learn from, and provide support to, other team members, while delivering top levels of service to all residents (essential). **Skills:** * **Communication Skills:** Both written and verbal, for interacting with residents, staff, and external stakeholders (essential) * **Problem\-Solving:** Strong abilities in addressing resident disputes (essential). * **Customer Service:** Demonstrated experience in delivering exceptional customer service (essential). * **Computer Literacy:** While training on our property management systems will be provided, we require that the applicant be proficient with Microsoft Office and Outlook and have an aptitude for learning new software (essential). **Qualifications (desired):** * Bachelor’s degree in business administration, or a related field (desired) * Full clean Irish driver’s licence **Remuneration/ Bonuses / Benefits** * Base salary:€37,400 * Bonus: Discretionary Bonus Scheme * Benefits: Private health insurance; Pension Scheme; Cycle to Work Scheme; Company Sick Pay. **Working Hours:** * 5\-day working week (40 hours) including weekends (Saturday and Sunday). * This role includes nighttime shift work (between 22:30 – 07:30\) **Location:** This is a client facing role and must be based on site at the rostered location(primarily at Glass Bottle, Sandymount Strand, Dublin 4 but may be required to work at other locations as required by the business). **Equal Opportunities Statement:** We are an Equal Opportunities Employer, committed to creating an inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplace. We provide equal opportunities for everyone and offer reasonable adjustments to ensure all candidates have a fair chance during the recruitment process. ### **Company Overview** CompassRock provides asset and property management services to the rental housing sectors. Originally founded in the U.S., the company has expanded to the UK and is rapidly growing its portfolio of Build\-to\-Rent (BTR) assets across the UK and Ireland. Backed by private equity, CompassRock is in an exciting growth phase, actively acquiring and developing new assets at a fast pace. With an entrepreneurial and innovative culture embedded in its DNA, we value the dynamic, forward\-thinking individuals it employs.

title: Partner Solutions Architect, WWPS Global ISV Partners
description: **DESCRIPTION** --------------- Amazon Web Services (AWS) is seeking a Solutions Architect to join the Worldwide Public Sector ISV Partner Solutions Architecture team. In this role, you will serve as the embedded technical partner for strategic ISV partners in the security and intelligence domain whose development teams are headquartered in Dublin and the broader EMEA region. Your primary mission: help these partners build, modernize, and scale their solutions on AWS — driving architecture quality, accelerating product roadmap delivery, and enabling joint go\-to\-market motions that create value for public sector customers worldwide. This is a builder\-oriented role. You will work directly alongside partner engineering teams on architecture design, solution modernization, and hands\-on integration with AWS services. You will translate partner product roadmaps into AWS\-native architectures, identify opportunities for service adoption, and serve as the technical bridge between ISV R\&D organizations and AWS product/specialist teams. Ability to travel within EMEA (\~30%) for partner site visits and customer engagements. Must be a citizen of the EU with work authorization. Key job responsibilities * Serve as the primary AWS technical resource embedded with ISV partner development teams in Dublin/EMEA, providing architecture guidance, code\-level integration support, and solution design for security and intelligence workloads. * Conduct architecture reviews and Well\-Architected assessments on partner solutions, identifying optimization opportunities across cost, reliability, performance, and security pillars. * Drive solution modernization initiatives — helping partners containerize, adopt serverless patterns, integrate AI/ML capabilities (Amazon Bedrock, SageMaker), and leverage managed services to reduce undifferentiated heavy lifting. * Support sovereign cloud and restricted environment deployments (EU Sovereign) by providing architectural guidance on data residency, compliance frameworks, and regional service availability. * Enable partner go\-to\-market readiness including AWS Marketplace listing optimization, consulting partner onboarding, APN competency and program enrollment. A day in the life * You spend your morning in a design session with a partner engineering team, whiteboarding a migration from monolithic on\-prem DLP architecture to a containerized, event\-driven pattern on ECS/EKS with S3 and Lambda. * After lunch, you join a cross\-functional call with AWS storage specialists to resolve an FSx configuration for a partner workload that requires specific IOPS in a restricted compliance boundary. * You draft a section of a joint reference architecture document showing how a partner’s threat intelligence platform integrates with Amazon Bedrock for agentic AI title: Partnerships and Business Development Intern
description: **About Merces:** Merces is Ireland's first fitness rewards app \- and the first of its kind in the world. Our technology integrates with Strava to connect runs and walks to real rewards from the best cafés, wellness brands and local businesses. We launched in Dublin in June 2026 and we're growing fast. **The Role:** We're looking for a driven, commercially sharp and people\-focused Partnerships and Business Development Intern to join the Merces team this summer. This is not a sit\-at\-a\-desk\-and\-send\-emails internship. This is getting out there, building real relationships, and playing a genuine role in the growth of something that has never been done before. **What you'll be doing:** * Identifying and reaching out to run clubs across Dublin to bring them onto the Merces platform * Researching and approaching corporate sponsors in Dublin for our Impact Challenge fundraising programme * Building and managing a pipeline of partnership leads * Supporting the Founder and CEO in preparing for and following up on partnership meetings * Logging activity and tracking progress in our partnerships tracker * Getting involved in events and activations as they happen over the summer **What we're looking for:** * Someone who is naturally good with people and not afraid to pick up the phone or send a cold DM * Commercially minded with an interest in business development or sales * Organised, proactive and self\-directed \- you will own your pipeline * Based in Dublin for the summer * Ideally studying Business, Marketing or Commerce at Third Level * A genuine interest in fitness, wellness or the startup world is a big plus **Why this role is different:** The health, fitness and wellness industry is one of the fastest growing industries in the world right now. Getting hands\-on experience in partnerships and business development at a funded Irish startup at this stage is something genuinely worth having on your CV. You will be building real relationships with real businesses from day one. Not photocopying. Not making coffee. **What you get:** * Real experience building the commercial side of a fast\-moving Irish startup from the ground up * Direct access to and mentorship from the founder * Involvement in launch events and activations across Dublin and potentially London * Expenses covered * A summer that actually looks good on your CV **To apply:** Send us a DM on Instagram @club\_merces or email info@mercesapp.com with your CV and a quick note on why you'd be great for this role.

title: Plant Operator I
description: **About Us** Stericycle is a U.S. based business\-to\-business services company and leading provider of compliance\-based solutions that protects people and brands, promotes health and well\-being, and safeguards the environment. Since our founding over 30 years ago, we have grown from a small start\-up in medical waste management into a leader across a range of increasingly complex and highly regulated arenas, serving healthcare organizations and commercial businesses of every size. Every day, we help our customers solve complex challenges by safely managing materials that could otherwise spread disease, contaminate the environment, or compromise one’s identity. Join us on our mission to protect health and well\-being in a safe, responsible, and sustainable way. **Position Purpose** The Plant Operative is responsible for the receipt, management, and transfer out of all waste in compliance with licence conditions and company procedures. **Key Job Activities** To receive, manage and transfer out all waste within the facility. To ensure bins are clean and serviceable and carry out maintenance of clinical waste bins To ensure waste is properly segregated on arrival and dispatch to and from the facility To load bulk waste trailers to meet the needs of the facility To load and unload waste into bins and containers to meet the needs of the facility Use and operate the waste tracking systems that are used by the company To ensure the highest standards of quality are maintained. To maintain a safe working environment. To work alongside outlined Standard Operational Procedures. To observe Health \& Safety Regulations, other relevant legislation and safe working practices. To report equipment faults and defects of any nature to the site management and rectify where possible. All other duties deemed reasonable to carry out duties with the job role. **Education** High School or equivalent. **Experience (EMEAA)** Preferred Experience: Basic knowledge of plant / warehouse operations. Able to work effectively under pressure. title: Portfolio Operations Associate
description: **Overview** Fisher Investments Ireland is the local entity of Fisher Investments, a privately held investment management firm that provides professional discretionary portfolio management services for high net worth individuals and some of the world's preeminent institutions. Fisher Investments' presence in Europe has grown significantly over the past decade, with a recent expansion into Ireland and Luxembourg. We're looking for someone with an analytical background to join our team as a Portfolio Operations Associate based in our Dublin office, and be a part of helping shape the future of Portfolio Operations at Fisher. **The Opportunity:** As a Portfolio Operations Associate at Fisher Investments Ireland, you will be part of Fisher's Portfolio Management Group. You will maintain investment strategies for our international retail accounts. Your primary responsibilities will involve generating account\-specific trade orders; helping coordinate and execute portfolio shifts, regularly working with Portfolio Operations Analysts, Portfolio Engineers and Traders; matching trades with counterparties; and processing foreign currency conversions. As a member of this newer team you will be encouraged to suggest strategic solutions to automate and scale our daily processes, increasing our efficiency while providing unparalleled service to our valued clients. **The Day\-to\-Day:** * Oversee trade order generation in new client accounts and existing client accounts to align with the appropriate strategy * Produce account level trade orders to implement strategy level changes * Facilitate prompt and accurate execution of client trade requests * Support client services by serving as a point of contact for questions related to individual client portfolios * Perform operational tasks related to client portfolio management * Help design, execute, and maintain scalable workflows and systems that support the future growth of our business **Your Qualifications:** * A university degree or equivalent combination of education and experience * No professional experience required, while some is favoured * Be able to follow operational workflows * A sound knowledge of financial concepts, markets, and current events * Experience implementing process improvements * Native or fluent English language skills. Proficiency in other European languages is beneficial but not required * Eagerness to help expand a growing team * Experience communicating on a team and able to work to a high level of detail **Why Fisher Investments Ireland:** The global Fisher organisation distinguishes itself by putting clients first, providing unmatched service, and taking a personalised approach to investing. You can feel confident knowing that Fisher Investments and Fisher Investments Ireland align with our clients’ priorities by using a simple and transparent fee structure and recognised European custodians. It’s the people that make the Fisher purpose possible, and to help our employees meet their long\-term goals, we offer an array of benefits, including: * 100% paid premiums for our top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top up to full base pay for eligible employees * $10,000\* fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy of up to €50 per month * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional wellbeing services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * This is an in\-office role. Based on your role, tenure, and performance eligibility you may have the opportunity to participate in our hybrid work from home program. This program is subject to change. * Employees residing outside of the US will be eligible for the $10,000 equivalent in their local currency. FISHER INVESTMENTS IRELAND IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

title: Pricing Analyst - French Entities M/F
description: Crédit Agricole Creditor Insurance (CACI) est une filiale de Crédit Agricole Assurances spécialisée dans l'assurance emprunteur et la protection de la vie. Leader du marché en France et 3e acteur en Europe, CACI sert plus de 5,6 millions de clients dans 6 pays. title: QA Engineer
description: **The Company** At AccountsIQ, we’re on a mission to deeply understand our customers and deliver technology that enriches their lives. We provide smart, feature\-rich financial management software designed for ambitious businesses that have outgrown basic starter systems or seek a simpler, more affordable alternative to complex, costly ERP solutions. Our cloud\-based accounting software transforms the finance function, making it easier for multi\-entity businesses to capture, process, and report their financial data with ease and efficiency. **The Role** This is a newly created QA Engineer role reporting to the Director of Engineering, driven by the continued growth of our engineering function. You will join an international team building and maintaining high\-quality SaaS products across web applications and backend services. Working within cross\-functional product squads in an Agile/Scrum environment, you will ensure quality is embedded throughout the development lifecycle. Automation and repeatability will be core to your work, with shared ownership of the quality of every software increment. **What you’ll be doing:** * Defining and executing testing scenarios across the full software development lifecycle * Building, maintaining, and improving automated UI tests using Playwright * Working closely with developers to clarify requirements and validate solutions * Performing and automating functional, regression, and acceptance testing * Tracking and reporting on quality metrics and investigating critical production issues * Promoting a strong quality\-first culture and mentoring junior QA team members **What you’ll need:** * 3\+ years’ experience in a QA or test automation role * Experience authoring and maintaining automated tests in Playwright with a code\-first approach * Experience working in Agile or Lean development environments * A solid understanding of test automation principles and quality best practices * Basic SQL knowledge and the ability to understand and work with C\# code * Experience with at least one scripting language * ISTQB certification (or equivalent) is a plus * You’re curious about new technologies and enjoy thoughtfully applying them to improve existing systems * Experience working on a SaaS product is a plus **What's the hiring process?** 1. A one\-way video interview via Hireflix, structured and reviewed manually by the hiring team, designed to understand how you think through practical QA scenarios such as exploratory testing, automation decisions, defect investigation, Playwright/test framework design, CI/CD, and release confidence. 2. A follow\-up call with our People team to discuss your application, motivations, availability, salary expectations, and any questions you have about the role or process. 3. A technical interview with the hiring team, consisting of a practical QA exercise. This includes a manual debugging task followed by writing an automation test in Playwright. 4. A final interview with our CTO. We use the one\-way video stage to give every candidate the same opportunity to answer role\-relevant QA questions, and all responses are reviewed manually by the hiring team. Please keep an eye out for an email from Hireflix, as this will be the first stage of the process following application review. **Why work with us?** AIQ Group is a multi\-product SaaS business, bringing together ExpenseIn and AccountsIQ, and recently ranked \#477 in the Financial Times FT1000 list of Europe’s fastest\-growing companies in 2026\. As a forward\-thinking and collaborative organisation, we combine drive, energy and ambition with a supportive, down\-to\-earth culture that enables people to do their best work. Our TEAM values (Together, Energy, Action, and Mindset) sit at the core of how we work and grow as a business. Following a €60M investment in 2024, it’s an exciting time to join the group as we continue to scale, enhance our products, and elevate the service we provide to our customers. **Some of our perks** * 28 days annual leave \+ bank holidays \+ your birthday off * Hybrid working * Working from abroad \- up to 6 weeks per calendar year * Private Health Insurance * 5% Pension contribution * Life Assurance \- 4 x Salary * Income Benefit * Wellbeing initiatives By submitting your application, you agree that AccountsIQ Group may collect your personal data for recruiting, global organisation planning, and related purposes. AccountsIQ Group’s Privacy Notice explains what personal information we may process, where we may process your personal information, our purposes for processing your personal information, and the rights you can exercise over AccountsIQ Group’s use of your personal information.
